objects.title: Slime mold
objects.extract: Slime molds (British English: Slime moulds) are a variety of small or microscopic organisms in different groups. They have both single-celled and multicellular forms during their life cycle, the individual cells coming together to form fruiting bodies that produce spores.

objects.title: Parfait
objects.extract: Parfait ( par-FAY, UK also  PAR-fay, French: [paʁfɛ] ; meaning "perfect") is either of two types of dessert. In France, where the dish originated, parfait is made by boiling cream, egg, sugar and syrup to create a custard-like or meringue-like puree which is then frozen.

objects.title: Göbekli Tepe
objects.extract: Göbekli Tepe (Turkish pronunciation: [ɟœbecˈli teˈpe]  'Potbelly Hill'; Kurdish: Girê Mirazan or Xerabreşkê, 'Wish Hill') is a Neolithic archaeological site in Upper Mesopotamia (al-Jazira) in modern-day Turkey. The settlement was inhabited from around 9500 BCE to at least 8000 BCE, during the Pre-Pottery Neolithic.

objects.title: Tapa cloth
objects.description: Barkcloth made in the island cultures of the Pacific Ocean
objects.extract: Tapa cloth (or simply tapa) is a barkcloth made in the islands of the Pacific Ocean, primarily in Tonga, Samoa and Fiji, but as far afield as Niue, Cook Islands, Futuna, Solomon Islands, Java, Sulawesi, New Zealand, Vanuatu, Papua New Guinea and Hawaii (where it is called kapa). In French Polynesia it has nearly disappeared, except for some villages in the M

objects.title: Ancient Greece
objects.description: Greek civilization from 1200 BC to 600 AD
objects.extract: Ancient Greece (Ancient Greek: Ἑλλάς, romanized: Hellás) was a northeastern Mediterranean civilization, existing from the Greek Dark Ages of the 12th–9th centuries BC to the end of classical antiquity (c. 600 AD), comprising a loose collection of culturally and linguistically related city-states and communities. Prior to the Roman period, most of these regio

objects.title: Maypole
objects.description: Tall wooden pole erected as a part of various European folk festivals
objects.extract:
A maypole is a tall wooden pole erected as a part of various European folk festivals, around which a maypole dance often takes place.
The festivals may occur on 1 May or Pentecost (Whitsun), although in some countries it is instead erected during Midsummer (20–26 June).

objects.title: Game & Watch
objects.description: Series of handheld electronic games by Nintendo
objects.extract: Game & Watch is a series of handheld electronic games developed by Nintendo. Designed by Gunpei Yokoi, the first game, Ball was released in 1980 and the original production run of the devices continued until 1991.

objects.title: Chopped liver
objects.description: Liver pâté popular in Ashkenazi Jewish cuisine
objects.extract: Chopped liver (Yiddish: געהאַקטע לעבער, gehakte leber) is a liver pâté popular in Ashkenazi Jewish cuisine. This dish is a common menu item in kosher Jewish delicatessens in Britain, Canada, South Africa, Argentina and the United States.

objects.title: Salmon
objects.extract: Salmon (; pl.: salmon) are any of several commercially important species of euryhaline ray-finned fish from the genera Salmo and Oncorhynchus of the family Salmonidae, native to tributaries of the North Atlantic (Salmo) and North Pacific (Oncorhynchus) basins.  Salmon is a colloquial or common name used for fish in this group, but is not a scientific name.

objects.title: Wildland fire engine
objects.description: Fire engine designed for fighting wildfires
objects.extract: A wildland fire engine or brush truck is a fire engine specifically designed to assist in fighting wildfires by transporting firefighters to the scene and providing them with access to the fire, along with water or other equipment. There are multiple types of wildfire apparatus which are used in different scenarios.

objects.title: Recycling
objects.description: Converting waste materials into new products
objects.extract: Recycling is the process of converting waste materials into new materials and objects. This concept often includes the recovery of energy from waste materials.

objects.title: Shoe
objects.extract:
A shoe is an item of footwear normally found in pairs intended to protect and comfort the human foot, usually made in such a way that one is designed to fit the left foot and the other the right foot.
Shoes are intended to provide further protection to the vulnerable human foot, though the foot is highly adaptable to various terrains.

objects.title: Newspaper
objects.description: Scheduled publication of information about current events
objects.extract: A newspaper is a periodical publication containing written information about current events and is often typed in black ink with a white or gray background. Newspapers can cover a wide variety of fields such as politics, business, sports, art, science, and religions.
objects.artist: Original uploader was Hmbr at he.wikipedia

objects.title: Diving bell
objects.description: Chamber for transporting divers vertically through the water
objects.extract: A diving bell is a rigid chamber used to transport divers from the surface to depth and back in open water, usually for the purpose of performing underwater work. The most common types are the open-bottomed wet bell and the closed bell, which can maintain an internal pressure greater than the external ambient.

objects.title: Zora spinimana
objects.extract: Zora spinimana is a prowling spider of the family Miturgidae with a Palearctic distribution.  It is the type species of the genus Zora.

objects.title: Miniopterus fuliginosus
objects.extract: The eastern bent-wing bat (Miniopterus fuliginosus) is a species of vesper bat in the family Miniopteridae. It is found in South Asia, Far-east Asia, the east Caucasus Mountains and also in Southeast Asian regions.

objects.title: chana masala
objects.description: Chickpea dish from the Indian subcontinent
objects.extract: Chana masala (also chole masala or chholay) is a chickpea curry cooked in a tomato-based sauce, originating from the Indian subcontinent. It is a staple dish in Indian (particularly North Indian) and Pakistani cuisine.

objects.title: Hyena
objects.extract: Hyenas or hyaenas ( hi-EE-nəz; from Ancient Greek ὕαινα, hýaina) are feliform carnivoran mammals belonging to the family Hyaenidae ( Latin pronunciation: [i.ɛː.ni.dɛː]). With just four extant species (each in its own genus), it is the fifth-smallest family in the order Carnivora and one of the smallest in the class Mammalia.

objects.title: Salty liquorice
objects.extract:
Salty liquorice, also known as salmiak liquorice, salmiac liquorice or (in Finland) salmiakki, is a variety of liquorice flavoured with salmiak salt (sal ammoniac; ammonium chloride), and is a common confection found in the Nordic countries, Benelux, and northern Germany. 
Salmiak salt gives salty liquorice an astringent, salty taste, akin to that of tannins

objects.title: Raccoon
objects.description: Medium-sized mammal native to North America
objects.extract: The raccoon ( or US:  , Procyon lotor), sometimes called the North American, northern or common raccoon (also spelled racoon) to distinguish it from other species of raccoon, is a mammal native to North America. It is the largest of the procyonid family, having a body length of 40 to 70 cm (16 to 28 in), and a body weight of 5 to 26 kg (11 to 57 lb).

objects.title: Fluidized bed
objects.extract: A fluidized bed is a physical phenomenon that occurs when a solid particulate substance (usually present in a holding vessel) is under the right conditions so that it behaves like a fluid. The usual way to achieve a fluidized bed is to pump pressurized fluid into the particles.

objects.title: Black tie
objects.extract: Black tie is a semi-formal Western dress code for evening events, originating in British and North American conventions for attire in the 19th century. In British English, the dress code is often referred to synecdochically by its principal element for men, the dinner suit or dinner jacket.

objects.title: Agate
objects.extract: Agate ( AG-it) is a banded variety of fibrous chalcedony. Agate stones are characterized by alternating bands of different colored chalcedony and often contain some visible quartz crystals.

objects.title: Soldering iron
objects.extract: A soldering iron is  a hand tool used in soldering. It supplies heat to melt solder so that it can flow into the joint between two workpieces.

objects.title: Egg timer
objects.extract: An egg timer or kitchen timer is a device whose primary function is to assist in timing during cooking; the name comes from the first timers initially being used for the timing of cooking eggs. Early designs simply counted down for a specific period of time.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Lumos3 assumed (based on copyright claims).

objects.title: Friedhofslinde in Stemwede-Oppenwehe
objects.description: very old linden tree at an churchyard in Stemwede-Oppenwehe (North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany)

objects.title: Wax sculpture
objects.extract: A wax sculpture is a depiction made using a waxy substance. Often these are effigies, usually of a notable individual, but there are also death masks and scenes with many figures, mostly in relief.

objects.title: Xenorhina thiekeorum
objects.artist: Günther R, Richards S (2021) Description of six new species of Xenorhina Peters,

objects.title: Hydroponics
objects.description: Growing plants without soil using nutrients in water
objects.extract: Hydroponics is a type of horticulture and a subset of hydroculture which involves growing plants, usually crops or medicinal plants, without soil, by using water-based mineral nutrient solutions in an artificial environment. Terrestrial or aquatic plants may grow freely with their roots exposed to the nutritious liquid or the roots may be mechanically suppor

objects.title: Dune buggy
objects.extract: A dune buggy, also known as a beach buggy, is a recreational off-road vehicle with large wheels, and wide tires, designed for use on sand dunes, beaches, off-road or desert recreation. The design is usually a topless vehicle with a rear-mounted engine.

objects.title: Taiko
objects.extract: Taiko (太鼓) are a broad range of Japanese percussion instruments. In Japanese, the term taiko refers to any kind of drum, but outside Japan, it is used specifically to refer to any of the various Japanese drums called wadaiko (和太鼓, lit. 'Japanese drums') and to the form of ensemble taiko drumming more specifically called kumi-daiko (組太鼓, lit. 'set of drums').

objects.title: Tempeh
objects.extract: Tempeh or tempe (; Javanese: ꦠꦺꦩ꧀ꦥꦺ, romanized: témpé, Javanese pronunciation: [tempe]) is a traditional Indonesian food made from fermented soybeans. It is made by a natural culturing and controlled fermentation process that binds soybeans into a cake form.

objects.title: Waffle
objects.extract: A waffle is a dish made from usually leavened batter or dough that is cooked between two plates that are patterned to give a characteristic size, shape, and surface impression. There are many variations based on the type of waffle iron and recipe used.
objects.artist: Parkerman & Christie from San Diego, USA

objects.title: Syncretism
objects.extract: Syncretism () is the combining or merging of various distinct beliefs or schools of thought, particularly religious ones. For instance, features and components of one religion are incorporated and absorbed into another.

objects.title: Radiated tortoise
objects.extract: The radiated tortoise (Astrochelys radiata) is a  tortoise species in the family Testudinidae. Although this species is native to and most abundant in southern Madagascar, it can also be found in the rest of this island, and has been introduced to the islands of Réunion and Mauritius.

objects.title: Lewis Hine
objects.extract: Lewis Wickes Hine (September 26, 1874 – November 3, 1940) was an American sociologist and muckraker photographer. His photographs taken during times such as the Progressive Era and the Great Depression captured young children working in harsh conditions, playing a role in bringing about the passage of the first child labor laws in the United States.

objects.title: ceviche
objects.extract: Ceviche, cebiche, sebiche, or seviche is a traditional cold dish consisting of raw fish or shellfish marinated in citrus and seasonings. Different versions of ceviche are part of the culinary cultures of various Latin American countries along the Pacific Ocean where each iteration is considered native, including Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, El Salva

objects.title: List of eggplant dishes
objects.extract: This is a list of dishes featuring eggplant as the main or an essential ingredient. Eggplant, also known as aubergine, is used in the cuisine of many countries.

objects.title: Ayam Cemani
objects.extract: The Ayam Cemani is a rare breed of chicken from Indonesia. They have a dominant gene that causes hyperpigmentation (fibromelanosis), making the chicken mostly black, including feathers, beak, and internal organs.

objects.title: Cabomba aquatica
objects.extract: Cabomba aquatica is a rhizomatous, perennial, aquatic herb in the family Cabombaceae native to South America. It occurs in fresh standing water or in lakes and rivers with slight currents.

objects.title: Hot rod
objects.description: American car with a large engine modified for linear speed
objects.extract:
Hot rods are typically American automobiles that have been rebuilt or modified with larger or more powerful engines optimized for speed and acceleration. One commonly cited description defines a hot rod as “a car that’s been stripped down, souped up, and made to go much faster.”
Beyond mechanical modification, hot rodding developed as a distinctive cultural

objects.title: Palaeoloxodon naumanni
objects.description: Extinct species of elephant native to Japan
objects.extract: Palaeoloxodon naumanni is an extinct species of elephant belonging to the genus Palaeoloxodon that was native to the Japanese archipelago during the Middle to Late Pleistocene. It is named after the German geologist Heinrich Edmund Naumann who first described remains of the species in the late 19th century, with the species sometimes being called Naumann's e

objects.title: Chestnut-bellied Guan
objects.extract: The chestnut-bellied guan (Penelope ochrogaster) is a species of bird in the family Cracidae. It is found only in Brazil.

objects.title: ANEC IV
objects.extract: The ANEC IV Missel Thrush was a 1920s British two-seat light aircraft built by Air Navigation and Engineering Company Limited at Addlestone Surrey.

objects.title: Bee
objects.extract: Bees are winged insects that form a monophyletic clade Anthophila within the superfamily Apoidea of the order Hymenoptera, with over 20,000 known species in seven recognized families. Some species – including honey bees, bumblebees, and stingless bees – are social insects living in highly hierarchical colonies, while over 90% of bee species – including mason

objects.title: Habronattus georgiensis
objects.extract: Habronattus georgiensis is a species in the family Salticidae (jumping spiders), found in Georgia and the USA.

objects.title: Drink can
objects.description: Container specifically made for liquid such as beverages and usually made of aluminum
objects.extract: A drink can (or beverage can) is a metal container with a polymer interior designed to hold a fixed portion of liquid such as carbonated soft drinks, alcoholic drinks, fruit juices, teas, herbal teas, energy drinks, etc. Drink can exteriors are made of aluminum (75% of worldwide production) or tin-plated steel (25% worldwide production) and the interiors coa

objects.title: Spotted rocksnail
objects.extract: The spotted rocksnail, scientific name Leptoxis picta, is a species of freshwater snail with a gill and an operculum, an aquatic gastropod mollusc in the family Pleuroceridae. This species is endemic to Alabama in the United States.

objects.title: Sherlock Holmes
objects.description: Fictional character created by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle
objects.extract: Sherlock Holmes () is a fictional detective created by British author Arthur Conan Doyle. Referring to himself as a "consulting detective" in his stories, Holmes is known for his proficiency with observation, deduction, forensic science and logical reasoning that borders on the fantastic, which he employs when investigating cases for a wide variety of client

objects.title: Megahexura fulva
objects.description: Genus of the spider M. fulva
objects.extract: Megahexura is a genus of spiders with the sole species Megahexura fulva, discovered in 1919. As of January 2026, it is the only genus in the family Megahexuridae.

objects.title: star anise
objects.description: fruit derived from the Illicium verum or badiam plant, used as a spice

objects.title: Pottery
objects.description: Craft of making objects from clay
objects.extract: Pottery is the process and the products of forming vessels and other objects with clay and other raw materials, which are fired at high temperatures to give them a hard and durable form. The place where a potter makes such wares is also called a pottery (plural potteries).

objects.title: Xiaomi MIX Fold 2
objects.extract: Xiaomi MIX Fold 2 is an Android-based foldable smartphone manufactured by Xiaomi. For the first time in the MIX Fold series, the phone developed in partnership with Leica camera, it was announced on August 11, 2022.

objects.title: Astronaut
objects.extract: An astronaut (from the Ancient Greek ἄστρον (astron), meaning 'star', and ναύτης (nautes), meaning 'sailor') is a person trained, equipped, and deployed by a human spaceflight program to serve as a commander or crew member of a spacecraft. Although generally reserved for professional space travelers, the term is sometimes applied to anyone who travels into s

objects.title: Eurytemora carolleeae
objects.artist: Natalia Sukhikh, Zoological Institute of RAS

objects.title: Mushroom
objects.description: Spore-bearing fruiting body of a fungus
objects.extract: A mushroom is the fleshy, spore-bearing fruiting body of a fungus, typically produced above ground on soil or another food source. A toadstool generally refers to a poisonous mushroom.
objects.artist: This image was created by user Dan Molter (shroomydan) at Mushroom Observer, a s

objects.title: Compact fluorescent lamp
objects.description: Fluorescent lamps with folded tubes, often with built-in ballast
objects.extract: A compact fluorescent lamp (CFL), also called compact fluorescent light, energy-saving light and compact fluorescent tube, is a fluorescent lamp designed to replace an incandescent light bulb; some types fit into light fixtures designed for incandescent bulbs. The lamps use a tube that is curved or folded to fit into the space of an incandescent bulb, and a

objects.title: Sichuan cuisine
objects.description: Cuisine originating from the Sichuan province of China
objects.extract: Sichuan cuisine or Sichuanese cuisine, alternatively romanized as Szechwan cuisine or Szechuan cuisine (Chinese: , Standard Mandarin pronunciation: [sɨ̂.ʈʂʰwán] ) is a style of Chinese cuisine originating from Sichuan province and the neighboring Chongqing municipality. Chongqing was formerly a part of Sichuan until 1997; thus, there is a great deal of cultu

objects.title: Hay
objects.description: Dried grass, legumes or other herbaceous plants used as animal fodder
objects.extract: Hay is grass, legumes, or other herbaceous plants that have been cut and dried to be stored for use as animal fodder, either for large grazing animals raised as livestock, such as cattle, horses, goats, and sheep, or for smaller domesticated animals such as rabbits and guinea pigs. Pigs can eat hay, but they do not digest it as efficiently as herbivores do.

objects.title: Watermill
objects.description: Structure that uses a water wheel or turbine
objects.extract: A watermill or water mill is a mill that uses hydropower. It is a structure that uses a water wheel or water turbine to drive a mechanical process such as milling (grinding), rolling, or hammering.

objects.title: Diamond Calk Horseshoe Company
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Hethurs assumed (based on copyright claims)

objects.title: FOS.98
objects.description: Coccosteus cuspidatus fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Britney Spears
objects.extract: Britney Jean Spears (born December 2, 1981) is an American singer. Referred to as the "Princess of Pop", she is widely regarded as one of the most influential entertainers of the 21st century.
objects.artist: U.S. Navy photo by Chief Warrant Officer 4 Seth Rossman.

objects.title: Chicken soup
objects.extract: Chicken soup is a soup made from chicken, simmered in water, usually with various other ingredients. The classic chicken soup consists of a clear chicken broth, often with pieces of chicken or vegetables; common additions are pasta, noodles, dumplings, carrots, potatoes, or grains such as rice and barley.

objects.title: Klong khaek
objects.extract: Klong khaek (Thai: กลองแขก, pronounced [klɔːŋ kʰɛ̀ːk]) is a type of double-headed barrel drum used in Thai music. The term literally means "Indian drum" from the words klong (กลอง) meaning drum and Khaek (แขก) meaning Indian or Tamil.

objects.title: Goblet drum
objects.extract: The goblet drum (also chalice drum, tarabuka, tarabaki, darbuka, darabuka, derbake, debuka,  doumbek, dumbec, dumbeg, dumbelek, toumperleki, tumbak, tabla, or zerbaghali; Arabic: دربوكة / Romanized: darbuka) is a single-head membranophone with a goblet-shaped body. It is most commonly used in the traditional music of North Africa and the Middle East.

objects.title: Zoo
objects.extract:
A zoo (short for zoological garden; also called a zoological park, animal park, or menagerie) is a facility where animals are kept within enclosures for exhibition and often bred for conservation purposes.
The term zoological garden refers to zoology, the study of animals.

objects.title: Cologna Veneta
objects.extract: Cologna Veneta is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 70 kilometres (43 mi) west of Venice and about 35 kilometres (22 mi) southeast of Verona. As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 8,207 and an area of 43.0 square kilometres (16.6 mi2).

objects.title: Incandescent light bulb
objects.description: Electric light bulb with a resistively heated wire filament
objects.extract: An incandescent light bulb, also known as an incandescent lamp or incandescent light globe, is an electric light that produces illumination by Joule heating a filament until it glows. The filament is enclosed in a glass bulb that is either evacuated or filled with inert gas to protect the filament from oxidation.

objects.title: Plywood
objects.description: Manufactured wood panel made from thin sheets of wood veneer
objects.extract: Plywood is a composite material manufactured from thin layers, or "plies", of wood veneer that have been stacked and glued together. It is an engineered wood from the family of manufactured boards, which include plywood, medium-density fibreboard (MDF), oriented strand board (OSB), and particle board (or chipboard).

objects.title: Sweetness
objects.extract: Sweetness is a basic taste most commonly perceived when eating foods rich in sugar.  Sweet tastes are generally regarded as pleasurable.

objects.title: Genoways's yellow bat
objects.extract: Genoways's yellow bat (Rhogeessa genowaysi) is a species of vesper bat found only in Mexico. It is threatened by habitat loss.

objects.title: Mortar and pestle
objects.extract: A mortar and pestle is a set of two simple tools used to prepare ingredients or substances by crushing and grinding them into a fine paste or powder in the kitchen, laboratory, and pharmacy. The mortar () is characteristically a bowl, typically made of hardwood, metal, ceramic, or hard stone such as granite.

objects.title: Flax
objects.extract: Flax, also known as common flax or line, is a flowering plant, Linum usitatissimum, in the family Linaceae. It is cultivated as a food and fiber crop in regions of the world with temperate climates.

objects.title: Acanthocidaris hastigera
objects.extract: Acanthocidaris hastigera is a species of sea urchin of the family Cidaridae. It was first described in 1907 by Alexander Emanuel Agassiz & Hubert Lyman Clark.

objects.title: Anglepoise lamp
objects.extract: The Anglepoise lamp is a balanced-arm lamp designed in 1932 by British designer George Carwardine.

objects.title: Jaguar
objects.description: Large cat native to the Americas
objects.extract: The jaguar (Panthera onca) is a large cat species and the only living member of the genus Panthera that is native to the Americas. Its distinctively marked coat features pale yellow to tan colored fur covered by spots that transition to rosettes on the sides, although a melanistic black coat appears in some individuals.

objects.title: Embarrassment
objects.description: Emotional state associated levels of discomfort
objects.extract: Embarrassment or awkwardness is an emotional state that is associated with mild to severe levels of discomfort, and which is usually experienced when someone commits (or thinks of) a socially unacceptable, or frowned-upon act that is witnessed by or revealed to others. Frequently grouped with shame and guilt embarrassment is considered a "self-conscious emot

objects.title: blanc de noirs
objects.description: white or rosé wine made from red or black grapes by separating the juice from the skins immediately

objects.title: Crown of thorns
objects.extract: According to the New Testament, a woven crown of thorns (Ancient Greek: στέφανος ἐξ ἀκανθῶν, romanized: stephanos ex akanthōn or ἀκάνθινος στέφανος, akanthinos stephanos) was placed on the head of Jesus during the events leading up to his crucifixion. It was one of the instruments of the Passion, employed by Jesus' captors both to cause him pain and to mock

objects.title: Magic wand
objects.description: magic wand at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 32.1.231)

objects.title: List of Japanese desserts and sweets
objects.extract: The Japanese had been making desserts for centuries before sugar was widely available in Japan. Many desserts commonly available in Japan can be traced back hundreds of years.

objects.title: Milestone
objects.description: Numbered marker along a road or boundary
objects.extract: A milestone is a numbered marker placed on a route such as a road, railway line, canal or boundary. They can indicate the distance to towns, cities, and other places or landmarks like mileage signs; or they can give their position on the route relative to some datum location (a zero milepost).

objects.title: Creamer
objects.description: cream pot at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 46.140.524)

objects.title: Polar Bear
objects.description: preserved Isle of Man steam locomotive
objects.extract: Polar Bear is a narrow-gauge steam locomotive built in 1905 by W. G. Bagnall for the Groudle Glen Railway. It is now preserved and runs on the Amberley Museum Railway.

objects.title: Platform Controller Hub
objects.extract: The Platform Controller Hub (PCH) is a family of Intel's single-chip chipsets, first introduced in 2009. It is the successor to the Intel Hub Architecture, which used two chips–a northbridge and southbridge, and first appeared in the Intel 5 Series.

objects.title: Alternative wine closure
objects.extract: Alternative wine closures are substitute closures used in the wine industry for sealing wine bottles in place of traditional cork closures. The emergence of these alternatives has grown in response to quality control efforts by winemakers to protect against "cork taint" caused by the presence of the chemical trichloroanisole (TCA).

objects.title: Lemon
objects.extract: The lemon (Citrus × limon) is a species of small evergreen tree in the Citrus genus of the flowering plant family Rutaceae. A true lemon is a hybrid of the citron and the bitter orange.

objects.title: Windmill
objects.description: Machine that makes use of wind energy
objects.extract: A windmill is a machine operated by the force of wind acting on vanes or sails to mill grain (gristmills). Windmills were used throughout the high medieval and early modern periods; the horizontal or panemone windmill first appeared in Persia during the 9th century, and the vertical windmill first appeared in northwestern Europe in the 12th century.

objects.title: Barley
objects.extract: Barley (Hordeum vulgare), a member of the grass family, is a major cereal grain grown in temperate climates globally. One of the first cultivated grains, it was domesticated in the Fertile Crescent around 9000 BC, giving it nonshattering spikelets and making it much easier to harvest.

objects.title: Wedding ring
objects.description: Finger ring which indicates that its wearer is married
objects.extract: A wedding ring or wedding band is a finger ring that indicates that its wearer is married. It is usually forged from metal, traditionally gold or another precious metal.

objects.title: Lavandula
objects.extract: Lavandula (common name lavender) is a genus of 47 known species of perennial flowering plants in the sage family, Lamiaceae. It is native to the Old World, primarily found across the drier, warmer regions of the Mediterranean, with an affinity for maritime breezes.

objects.title: Othello
objects.description: Play by William Shakespeare written circa 1603
objects.extract: The Tragedy of Othello, the Moor of Venice, often shortened to Othello, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare around 1603. Set in Venice and Cyprus, the play depicts the Moorish military commander Othello as he is manipulated by his ensign, Iago, into suspecting his wife Desdemona of infidelity.

objects.title: Watch
objects.extract: A watch is a timepiece carried or worn by a person. It is designed to maintain a consistent movement despite the motions caused by the person's activities.

objects.title: Pickelhaube
objects.description: Spiked helmet of the Prussian and German military
objects.extract: The Pickelhaube (German: [ˈpɪkl̩ˌhaʊ̯bə] ; pl. Pickelhauben [ˈpɪkl̩ˌhaʊ̯bn̩] ), also Pickelhelm, is a spiked leather or metal helmet that was worn in the 19th and 20th centuries by Prussian and German soldiers of all ranks as well as firefighters and police.

objects.title: List of British cheeses
objects.extract: This is a list of cheeses from the United Kingdom.

objects.title: Hot dog
objects.extract: A hot dog is a grilled, steamed, or boiled sausage served in the slit of a partially sliced bun. The term hot dog can also refer to the sausage itself.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Diderot~commonswiki assumed (based on copyr

objects.title: Kinixys nogueyi
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Diotime75 assumed (based on copyright claim

objects.title: Tape measure
objects.description: Flexible ruler used to measure size or distance
objects.extract: A tape measure or measuring tape is a long, flexible ruler used to measure length or distance. It usually consists of a ribbon of cloth, plastic, fibreglass, or metal (usually - hard steel alloy) strip with linear measurement markings.

objects.title: Chicken soup
objects.extract: Chicken soup is a soup made from chicken, simmered in water, usually with various other ingredients. The classic chicken soup consists of a clear chicken broth, often with pieces of chicken or vegetables; common additions are pasta, noodles, dumplings, carrots, potatoes, or grains such as rice and barley.

objects.title: Quince cheese
objects.extract: Quince cheese (also known as quince paste) is a sweet and tart, thick jelly made of the pulp of the quince fruit. It is a common confection in several countries.

objects.title: Hairy Frog
objects.extract: The hairy frog (Trichobatrachus robustus), also known as the horror frog or Wolverine frog, is a Central African species of frog in the family Arthroleptidae. It is typically considered monotypic within the genus Trichobatrachus, but based on its genetics, it should be included in Astylosternus instead.

objects.title: Children's clothing
objects.extract: Children's clothing or kids' clothing is clothing for children who have not yet grown to full height. Children's clothing is often more casual than adult clothing, fit for play and rest.
objects.artist: Adam Jones from Kelowna, BC, Canada

objects.title: Horse racing
objects.extract: Horse racing is an equestrian performance activity, typically involving two or more horses ridden by jockeys (or sometimes driven without riders) over a set distance for competition. It is one of the most ancient of all sports, as its basic premise – to identify which of two or more horses is the fastest over a set course or distance – has been mostly unchan
objects.artist: Paul VanDerWerf from Brunswick, Maine, USA

objects.title: Battleship (game)
objects.description: Strategy type guessing game for two players
objects.extract: Battleship (also known as Battleships) is a strategy type guessing game for two players. It is played on ruled grids (paper or board) on which each player's fleet of warships are marked.
objects.artist:
I would appreciate being notified if you use my work outside Wikimedia.
Do not c

objects.title: Goat milk
objects.description: Liquid food produced by female goats
objects.extract: Goat milk is the milk of domestic goats. Goats produce about 2% of the world's total annual milk supply.

objects.title: Santos-Dumont No. 5 airship
objects.artist: José Rosael/Hélio Nobre/Museu Paulista da USP

objects.title: Lactarius griseus
objects.artist: This image was created by user walt sturgeon (Mycowalt) at Mushroom Observer, a

objects.title: Machine gun
objects.extract: A machine gun (MG) is a fully automatic and rifled firearm designed for sustained direct fire. Automatic firearms of 20 mm (0.79 in) caliber or more are usually classified as autocannons rather than machine guns.

objects.title: Plaquette
objects.description: Type of small low relief sculpture
objects.extract: A plaquette (French: [plakɛt]; "small plaque") is a small low relief sculpture in bronze or other materials. These were popular in Italian Renaissance sculpture and later.

objects.title: Skirt
objects.description: Clothing worn from the waist or hips
objects.extract:
A skirt is the lower part of a dress or a separate outer garment that covers a person from the waist downwards.
At its simplest, a skirt can be a draped garment made out of a single piece of fabric (such as pareos).

objects.title: Cactus
objects.description: Family of mostly succulent plants, adapted to dry environments
objects.extract: A cactus (pl.: cacti, cactuses, or less commonly, cactus) is a member of the plant family Cactaceae (), a family of the order Caryophyllales comprising about 127 genera with some 1,750 known species. The word cactus derives, through Latin, from the Ancient Greek word κάκτος (káktos), a name originally used by Theophrastus for a spiny plant whose identity is
objects.artist:
Owoce_Saguaro.jpg: *Carnegiea gigantea top.jpg: User:Stan Shebs
Saguaro 01.jpg:

objects.title: Pineapple
objects.description: Species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae
objects.extract:
The pineapple (Ananas comosus) is a tropical plant with an edible fruit; it is the most economically significant plant in the family Bromeliaceae.
The pineapple is native to South America, where it has been cultivated for many centuries.

objects.title: Drum
objects.description: drum displayed in the Genadendal Museum

objects.title: Lichen
objects.extract: A lichen ( LY-kən, UK also  LITCH-ən) is a hybrid colony of algae or cyanobacteria living symbiotically among filaments of multiple fungus species, along with bacteria embedded in the cortex, or "skin", in a mutualistic relationship. Lichens are the lifeform that first brought the term symbiosis (as Symbiotismus) into biological context.

objects.title: Partisan of the Galleys
objects.description: object in Musée national de la Marine

objects.title: Toyota Corolla
objects.extract: The Toyota Corolla (Japanese: トヨタ・カローラ, Hepburn: Toyota Karōra) is a series of compact cars (formerly subcompact) manufactured and marketed globally by the Japanese automaker Toyota Motor Corporation since 1966. Since 1997, the Corolla has remained the best-selling automobile nameplate of all time, surpassing the Volkswagen Beetle.

objects.title: Tree shaping
objects.description: Use of living trees to create structures and art
objects.extract: Tree shaping (also known by several other alternative names) uses living trees and other woody plants as the medium to create structures and art. There are a few different methods used by the various artists to shape their trees, which share a common heritage with other artistic horticultural and agricultural practices, such as pleaching, bonsai, espalier, a

objects.title: Marimba
objects.extract: The marimba ( mə-RIM-bə) is a musical instrument in the percussion family that consists of wooden bars that are struck by mallets. Below each bar is a resonator pipe that amplifies particular harmonics of its sound.

objects.title: Dieppe
objects.description: Subprefecture and commune in Normandy, France
objects.extract: Dieppe (French pronunciation: [djɛp]; Norman: Dgieppe; prob. from Old English  dēōp or Old Norse djúpr 'deep') is a coastal commune in the Seine-Maritime department, Normandy, northern France.

objects.title: List of lamb dishes
objects.extract: This is a list of the popular lamb and mutton dishes and foods worldwide. Lamb and mutton are terms for the meat of domestic sheep (species Ovis aries) at different ages.

objects.title: Ladybird of Szeged
objects.extract: The Ladybird of Szeged or Ladybug of Szeged (Hungarian: Szegedi Katicabogár or just Szegedi Katica) is an early robot which was developed in Hungary at the Cybernetics Laboratory of the University of Szeged (SZTE) between 1956 and 1957. The roughly hemispherical machine has a red shell with black dots, resembling a ladybird (or ladybug), hence its name.
objects.artist: Török Dániel (Transferred from hu.wikipedia to Commons by rlevente.)

objects.title: Traffic light
objects.description: Signalling device to control competing flows of traffic
objects.extract:
Traffic lights, traffic signals, or stoplights – also known as robots in South Africa, Zambia, and Namibia – are signalling devices positioned at road intersections, pedestrian crossings, and other locations to control the flow of traffic.
Traffic lights usually consist of three signals, conveying meaningful information to road users through colours and symb

objects.title: High-visibility clothing
objects.extract: High-visibility clothing (often shortened to hi vis or hi viz) is any clothing worn that is photoluminescent (e.g., fluorescent), highly reflective, or of a color that is easily discernible from any background. It is most commonly worn on the torso and arm area of the body.

objects.title: Box
objects.extract: A box (plural: boxes) is a container with rigid sides used for the storage or transportation of its contents. Most boxes have flat, parallel, rectangular sides (typically rectangular prisms).

objects.title: desert bandicoot
objects.extract: The desert bandicoot (Perameles eremiana) is an extinct bandicoot of the arid country in the centre of Australia.

objects.title: Paiban
objects.extract: The paiban (Chinese: 拍板; pinyin: pāibǎn) is a clapper made from several flat pieces of hardwood or bamboo (or, formerly, sometimes also ivory or metal), which is used in many different forms of Chinese music. There are many different types of paiban, and the instrument is also referred to as bǎn (板), tánbǎn (檀板; 'sandalwood clapper'), mùbǎn (木板; 'wooden clap

objects.title: Furniture
objects.description: Objects used to support human activities
objects.extract: Furniture refers to objects intended to support various human activities, such as seating (e.g., stools, chairs, and sofas), eating (tables), storing items, working, and sleeping (e.g., beds and hammocks). Furniture is also used to hold objects at a convenient height for work (as horizontal surfaces above the ground, such as tables and desks), or to store th

objects.title: Body proportions
objects.description: Proportions of the human body in art
objects.extract: Body proportions is the study of artistic anatomy, which attempts to explore the relation of the elements of the human body to each other and to the whole. These ratios are used in depictions of the human figure and may become part of an artistic canon of body proportion within a culture.

objects.title: Fork
objects.extract: In cutlery or kitchenware, a fork (from Latin: furca 'pitchfork') is a utensil, now usually made of metal, whose long handle terminates in a head that branches into several narrow and often slightly curved tines with which one can spear foods either to hold them to cut with a knife or to lift them to the mouth.

objects.title: CD player
objects.description: Electronic device that plays audio compact discs
objects.extract: A CD player is an electronic device that plays audio compact discs, which are a digital optical disc data storage format. CD players were first sold to consumers in 1982.

objects.title: Coyote
objects.description: Species of canine native to North America
objects.extract: The coyote /kaɪ(j)oʊt(i)/ (Canis latrans) is a species of canine also known as the American jackal, prairie wolf, and brush wolf. It is native to North America, and it is smaller than its close relative, the gray wolf, and slightly smaller than the closely related eastern wolf and red wolf.

objects.title: Street sweeper
objects.description: Machine or person that cleans streets in urban areas
objects.extract:
A street sweeper or street cleaner is a person or machine that cleans streets.
People have worked in cities as "sanitation workers" since sanitation and waste removal became a priority.

objects.title: Pitcher (container)
objects.description: Container with a spout used for storing and pouring liquids
objects.extract: In American English, a pitcher is a container with a handle and a spout, used for storing and pouring liquids. In English-speaking countries outside North America, such a container is called a jug.

objects.title: Hockey jersey
objects.description: Clothing worn by ice hockey players
objects.extract: A hockey jersey is a piece of clothing worn by ice hockey players to cover the upper part of their bodies. They also are worn by fans to show support for a team, or to create ties to a hometown or region.

objects.title: Wild boar
objects.extract: The wild boar (Sus scrofa), also known as the wild swine, common wild pig, Eurasian wild pig, or simply wild pig, is a suid native to much of Eurasia and North Africa, and has been introduced to the Americas and Oceania. The species is now one of the widest-ranging mammals in the world, as well as the most widespread suiform.

objects.title: Grey-hooded Warbler
objects.extract: The grey-hooded warbler (Phylloscopus xanthoschistos) is a species of leaf warbler (family Phylloscopidae). It is most famous for the way it warbles.

objects.title: Shellfish
objects.description: Culinary and fisheries term for exoskeleton-bearing aquatic invertebrates
objects.extract: Shellfish, in colloquial and fisheries usage, are exoskeleton-bearing aquatic invertebrates used as food, including various species of molluscs, crustaceans, and echinoderms. Although most kinds of shellfish are harvested from saltwater environments, some are found in freshwater.
objects.artist: The original uploader was Y6y6y6 at English Wikipedia.

objects.title: Scapolite-MGL 68902 90230
objects.description: facetted scapolite gemstone found in Merelani, Umba, Tanzania

objects.title: Concrete pump
objects.description: Machine used for transferring liquid concrete by pumping
objects.extract: A concrete pump is a machine used for transferring liquid concrete by pumping. There are different types of concrete pumps.

objects.title: Nakajima Ki-115
objects.extract: The Nakajima Ki-115 Tsurugi (剣; "sabre") is a single-seat aircraft that was developed specifically for kamikaze use by the Imperial Japanese Army Air Force during the closing stages of World War II in 1945. The Imperial Japanese Navy called this aircraft Tōka (藤花, "Wisteria Blossom").

objects.title: Paper mill
objects.extract: A paper mill is a factory devoted to making paper from vegetable fibers such as wood pulp, old rags, and other ingredients. Before the invention and adoption of the Fourdrinier machine and other types of paper machine that use an endless belt, all paper in a paper mill was made by hand, one sheet at a time, by specialized laborers.

objects.title: Coach (carriage)
objects.extract: Coaches are horse-drawn carriages which are large, enclosed, four-wheeled, pulled by two or more horses, and controlled by a coachman or postilion (riders). If driven by a coachman, there is a raised seat in front for a coachman called a box, box seat, or coach box.

objects.title: Pneumatics
objects.description: Use of pressurised gas in mechanical systems
objects.extract:
Pneumatics (from Greek πνεῦμα pneuma 'wind, breath') is the use of gas or pressurized air to create mechanical motion in mechanical systems.
Pneumatic systems used in industry are commonly powered by compressed air or compressed inert gases.

objects.title: Lactarius petersenii
objects.artist: This image was created by user I. G. Safonov (IGSafonov) at Mushroom Observer, a

objects.title: Mayfly
objects.description: Aquatic insects of the order Ephemeroptera
objects.extract: Mayflies (also up-winged flies or up-wing flies, or drake-flies in the UK; shadflies or fishflies in  northern U.S. and Canada) are aquatic insects belonging to the order Ephemeroptera. This order is part of an ancient group of insects termed the Palaeoptera, which also contains dragonflies and damselflies.

objects.title: Charles Rohlfs
objects.description: American furniture maker representative of the Arts and Crafts movement
objects.extract: Charles Rohlfs (February 15, 1853 – June 30, 1936), was an American actor, patternmaker, stove designer and furniture maker.  Rohlfs was a representative of the Arts and Crafts Movement, and was most famous for his skill as a furniture designer and maker.

objects.title: Guldengroschen
objects.extract:
The Guldengroschen or Guldiner was a large silver coin originally minted in Tirol in 1486, but which was introduced into the Duchy of Saxony in 1500.
The name "Guldengroschen" came from the fact that it has an equivalent denomination value in silver relative to that of the goldgulden (60 kreuzer).

objects.title: Physical restraint
objects.extract: Physical restraint refers to means of limiting or obstructing the freedom of a person's or an animal's bodily movement.

objects.title: Maize
objects.description: Species of grass cultivated as a food crop
objects.extract: Maize (; Zea mays), also known as corn, is a tall stout grass that produces cereal grain. The leafy stalk of the plant gives rise to male inflorescences or tassels which produce pollen, and female inflorescences called ears.

objects.title: U-110
objects.extract: German submarine U-110 was a Type IXB U-boat of Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine that operated during World War II. She was captured by the Royal Navy on 9 May 1941 and provided a number of secret cipher documents to the British. U-110's capture, later given the code name "Operation Primrose", was one of the biggest secrets of the war, remaining so for seven mont

objects.title: Huemmelchen
objects.extract:
The hümmelchen is a type of small German bagpipe, attested in Syntagma Musicum by Michael Praetorius during the Renaissance.
Early versions are believed to have double-reeded chanters, most likely with single-reeded drones.
objects.artist: Sönke Kraft aka Arnulf zu Linden

objects.title: Swatch
objects.extract: Swatch is a Swiss watch company founded in 1983 by Ernst Thomke, Elmar Mock, and Jacques Müller. It is a subsidiary of the Swatch Group.

objects.title: Peach
objects.description: Type of fruit tree, or its fruit
objects.extract: The peach (Prunus persica) is a deciduous tree that bears edible juicy fruits with various characteristics. Most are simply called peaches, while the glossy-skinned, non-fuzzy varieties are called nectarines.

objects.title: Aloe
objects.extract: Aloe (; also written Aloë) is a genus containing over 650 species of flowering succulent plants. The most widely known  species is Aloe vera, or "true aloe".

objects.title: Georgian era
objects.description: Historical period in Britain from 1714 to c. 1830–37
objects.extract: The Georgian era was a period in British history from 1714 to 1830, named after the Hanoverian kings George I, George II, George III and George IV. The definition of the Georgian era is also often extended to include the relatively short reign of William IV, which ended with his death in 1837. The subperiod that is the Regency era is defined by the regency o

objects.title: Dansby Swanson
objects.extract: James Dansby Swanson (born February 11, 1994) is an American professional baseball shortstop for the Chicago Cubs of Major League Baseball (MLB). He has previously played in MLB for the Atlanta Braves.
objects.artist: All-Pro Reels from District of Columbia, USA

objects.title: Loveseat
objects.extract:
A loveseat can be one of two styles of two-seat chair.
One form – also known as "British two-seaters" – is essentially synonymous with "two-seat couch".

objects.title: FS 835.106
objects.description: locomotive in the Peppone e Don Camillo Museum, Brescello, Italy

objects.title: Fazer
objects.extract: Fazer (Finnish pronunciation: [ˈfɑtser], Finland Swedish pronunciation: [ˈfat͡sær]) is one of the largest corporations in the Finnish food industry. The company was founded by Karl Fazer in 1891, as a "French-Russian café" in central Helsinki.

objects.title: Batik
objects.extract: Batik is a dyeing technique using wax resist. The term is also used to describe patterned textiles created with that technique.

objects.title: Black-capped Piprites
objects.extract: The black-capped piprites (Piprites pileata), also known as the black-capped manakin, bailarín castaño, or piprites capirotado, is a species of suboscine passerine. It has been placed in the genus Piprites, part of the Tyrannidae family.

objects.title: Chambéry
objects.description: Prefecture and city in Savoie, France
objects.extract:
Chambéry (UK: , US: , French: [ʃɑ̃beʁi]; Arpitan: Chambèri; Italian: Ciamberì) is the prefecture and largest city of the Savoie department in the southeastern Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France.
The population of the commune of Chambéry was 60,251 as of 2022, while the population of the Chambéry metropolitan area was 263,919.

objects.title: Lego Mindstorms
objects.description: Hardware and software platform by Lego
objects.extract: Lego Mindstorms (sometimes stylized as LEGO MINDSTORMS) is a discontinued line of educational kits for building programmable robots based on Lego bricks. It was introduced on 1 September 1998 and discontinued on 31 December 2022.

objects.title: Pack animal
objects.description: Individual or type of working animal used by humans
objects.extract:
A pack animal, also known as a sumpter animal or beast of burden, is a working animal used to transport goods or materials by carrying them, usually on its back.
Domestic animals of many species are used in this way, among them alpacas, Bactrian camels, donkeys, dromedaries, gaur, goats, horses, llamas, mules, reindeer, water buffaloes and yaks.

objects.title: Carbon capture and storage
objects.description: Process of capturing and storing carbon dioxide from industrial flue gas
objects.extract: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) is a process by which carbon dioxide (CO2) from industrial installations or natural sources is separated before it is released into the atmosphere, then transported to a long-term storage location. The CO2 is captured from a large point source, such as a natural gas processing plant and is typically stored in a deep geologica
objects.artist: Matt Hrkac from Geelong / Melbourne, Australia

objects.title: Water distribution system
objects.description: Infrastructure to carry potable water to consumers
objects.extract: A water distribution system is a part of water supply network with components that carry potable water from a centralized treatment plant or wells to consumers to satisfy residential, commercial, industrial and fire fighting requirements.

objects.title: Easter egg
objects.description: Decorated egg for the celebration of Easter
objects.extract: Easter eggs, also called Paschal eggs, are eggs that are decorated for the Christian festival of Easter, which celebrates the resurrection of Jesus. As such, Easter eggs are commonly used during the season of Eastertide (Easter season).

objects.title: Decorative box
objects.extract: A decorative box is a form of packaging that is generally more than just functional, but also intended to be decorative and artistic. Many such boxes are used for promotional packaging, both commercially and privately.

objects.title: Burqa
objects.description: Garment worn by some Muslim women
objects.extract:
A burqa, also known as harabah, is a type of enveloping outer garment worn by some Muslim women which fully covers the body and the face.
The use of face veils has been documented in various ancient cultures, including the Byzantine Empire, Persia, and Arabia.

objects.title: Priest
objects.description: Person authorized to lead the sacred rituals of a religion
objects.extract: A priest is a religious leader authorized to perform the sacred rituals of a religion, especially as a mediatory agent between humans and one or more deities. They also have the authority or power to administer religious rites; in particular, rites of sacrifice to, and propitiation of, a deity or deities.

objects.title: Bending machine (manufacturing)
objects.extract: A bending machine is a forming machine tool (DIN 8586). Its purpose is to assemble a bend on a workpiece.

objects.title: Human-powered land vehicle
objects.description: Vehicle propelled over ground by human power
objects.extract: Human-powered land vehicles are land vehicles propelled over ground by human power, The main ways to support the weight of a human-powered land vehicle and its contents above the ground are rolling contact; sliding contact; intermittent contact; no contact at all as with anything carried; or some combination of the above. The main methods of using human powe

objects.title: Llama
objects.extract:
The llama (; Spanish pronunciation: [ˈʎama] or [ˈʝama]) (Lama glama) is a domesticated South American camelid, widely used as a meat and pack animal by Andean cultures since the pre-Columbian era.
Llamas are social animals and live with others as a herd.

objects.title: Compactor
objects.extract: A compactor is a machine or mechanism used to reduce the size of material such as waste material or biomass through compaction. A trash compactor is used in business and public places like hospitals (and in the United States also in homes) to reduce the volume of trash they produce.
objects.artist: United States Air Force, MSgt. Rickie D. Bickle

objects.title: Stoneware
objects.description: Pottery fired at a relatively high temperature
objects.extract: Stoneware is a broad class of pottery fired at a relatively high temperature, to be impervious to water. A modern definition is a vitreous or semi-vitreous ceramic made primarily from stoneware clay or non-refractory fire clay.
objects.artist: Arthur B. Barlow (England, active 1871-1878), Doulton & Co. (England, London

objects.title: Versace
objects.description: Italian luxury fashion house in Milan
objects.extract: Gianni Versace S.r.l. (Italian: [ˈdʒanni verˈsaːtʃe]), usually referred to as Versace ( vər-SAH-chay), is an Italian luxury elite fashion company founded by Gianni Versace in 1978.

objects.title: Pressure measurement
objects.extract: Pressure measurement is the measurement of an applied force per unit area by a fluid (liquid or gas) on a surface. Pressure is typically expressed in units of pascals in the International System of Units (SI).

objects.title: Dune buggy
objects.extract: A dune buggy, also known as a beach buggy, is a recreational off-road vehicle with large wheels, and wide tires, designed for use on sand dunes, beaches, off-road or desert recreation. The design is usually a topless vehicle with a rear-mounted engine.

objects.title: Hammock
objects.description: Sling used for swinging, sleeping or resting
objects.extract: A hammock, from Spanish hamaca, borrowed from Taíno and Arawak hamaka, is a sling made of fabric, rope, or netting, suspended between two or more points, used for swinging, sleeping, or resting. It normally consists of one or more cloth panels, or a woven network of twine or thin rope stretched with ropes between two firm anchor points such as trees or posts

objects.title: Necktie
objects.description: Clothing item worn around the neck
objects.extract: A necktie (American English) – also called a long tie or, more usually, simply a tie (Commonwealth English) – is a cloth article of formal neckwear or office attire worn for decorative or symbolic purposes, knotted at the throat, resting under a folded shirt collar, and usually draped down the chest. On rare occasions, neckties are worn above a winged shirt

objects.title: List of Japanese desserts and sweets
objects.extract: The Japanese had been making desserts for centuries before sugar was widely available in Japan. Many desserts commonly available in Japan can be traced back hundreds of years.

objects.title: Cyryl the Independent Cat
objects.extract: Cyryl the Independent Cat (Polish pronunciation: [ˈt͡sɘ̟.rɘ̟l]; Polish: Niezależny Kot Cyryl), also known as the Independent Cat Monument (Polish: Pomnik Niezależnego Kota), is a bronze statue of a cat in Warsaw, Poland, placed in the Balaton Park, within the district of Praga-South. It was designed by Bogna Czechowska, and unveiled on 29 May 2011.

objects.title: Condiment
objects.description: Substance added to food for flavour
objects.extract: A condiment is a preparation that is added to food, typically after cooking, to enhance the flavor, to complement the dish, or to impart a specific flavor. Such specific flavors generally add sweetness or pungency, or sharp or piquant flavors.

objects.title: Bible
objects.extract: A Bible is a collection of religious texts that is central to Christianity or Judaism, and esteemed in other Abrahamic religions such as Islam. Bibles are anthologies (compilations of texts of a variety of forms) with varying canonical texts across traditions.

objects.title: Thomas Chippendale
objects.extract: Thomas Chippendale (June 1718 – 1779) was an English woodworker in London, designing furniture in the mid-Georgian, English Rococo, and Neoclassical styles. In 1754 he published a book of his designs in a trade catalogue titled The Gentleman and Cabinet Maker's Director—the most important collection of furniture designs published in England to that point whi

objects.title: Cricket (insect)
objects.description: Small insects of the family Gryllidae
objects.extract: Crickets are orthopteran insects which are related to bush crickets and, more distantly, to grasshoppers. In older literature, such as Imms, "crickets" were placed at the family level (i.e.

objects.title: FOS.175
objects.description: Psammodus rugosus fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Technology
objects.description: Use of knowledge for practical goals
objects.extract: Technology is the application of conceptual knowledge to achieve practical goals, especially in a reproducible way. The word technology can also mean the products resulting from such efforts, including both tangible tools such as utensils or machines, and intangible ones such as software.

objects.title: Ophicleide
objects.extract: The ophicleide ( OFF-ih-klyde) is a family of conical-bore keyed brass instruments invented in early 19th-century France to extend the keyed bugle into the lower range. Of these, the bass ophicleide in eight-foot (8′) C or 9′ B♭ took root over the course of the 19th century in military bands and as the bass of orchestral brass sections throughout Western Eur

objects.title: Tempeh
objects.extract: Tempeh or tempe (; Javanese: ꦠꦺꦩ꧀ꦥꦺ, romanized: témpé, Javanese pronunciation: [tempe]) is a traditional Indonesian food made from fermented soybeans. It is made by a natural culturing and controlled fermentation process that binds soybeans into a cake form.

objects.title: Harmoneon
objects.extract: The Harmoneon or concert accordion (French: Harmonéon, accordeon de concert) is a French free reed aerophone, invented by Pierre Monichon in 1948, although he only patented the instrument four years later in 1952. It has been taught in musical conservatories since 1974.

objects.title: Excavator
objects.extract:
Excavators are heavy construction equipment primarily consisting of a boom, dipper (or stick), bucket, and cab on a rotating platform known as the "house".
The modern excavator's house sits atop an undercarriage with tracks or wheels, being an evolution of the steam shovel (which itself evolved into the power shovel when steam was replaced by diesel and elec

objects.title: Baseball uniform
objects.description: Type of uniform worn by baseball players
objects.extract:
A baseball uniform is a type of uniform worn by baseball players, coaches and managers. 
Most baseball uniforms have the names and uniform numbers of players who wear them, usually on the backs of the uniforms to distinguish players from each other.

objects.title: Burgoo
objects.extract: Burgoo is a stew, similar to Irish or Mulligan stew, often served with cornbread or corn muffins, one form of which originated in Kentucky.  It is often prepared communally as a social gathering.

objects.title: Motocross
objects.description: Off-road motorcycle racing held on enclosed off-road circuits
objects.extract: Motocross (abbr. MX) is a form of off-road motorcycle racing held on enclosed off-road circuits.

objects.title: Aeroprakt A-32 Vixxen
objects.extract: The Aeroprakt A-32 is a Ukrainian two-seat, high-wing, tricycle gear ultralight aircraft that was designed by Yuri Yakovlev and is manufactured by Aeroprakt. In Australia the A-32 is referred to as the Vixxen.

objects.title: dulce de batata
objects.extract: Dulce de batata (Spanish: sweet potato candy) is a traditional South American jellied dessert. Depending on the locality, it is known by a variety of names, including dulce de boniatos and, in Brazil, marrom glacê (Portuguese: candied chestnut) or doce de batata doce.

objects.title: Bodhrán
objects.extract: The bodhrán (, Irish: [ˈbˠəuɾˠaːnˠ]; plural bodhráin) is a  frame drum used in Irish music ranging from 25 to 65 cm (10–26 in) in diameter, with most drums measuring 35–45 cm (14–18 in). The sides of the drum are 9–20 cm (3+1⁄2–8 in) deep.

objects.title: Atari
objects.extract: Atari () is a brand name that has been owned by several entities since its inception in 1972. It is currently owned by French holding company Atari SA (formerly Infogrames) and its focus is on "video games, consumer hardware, and licensing".

objects.title: Armband
objects.description: Piece of material worn around the arm
objects.extract: An armband is a piece of material worn around the arm. They may be worn for pure ornamentation, or to mark the wearer as belonging to group, or as insignia having a certain rank, status, office or role, or being in a particular state or condition.
objects.artist: Alfonso Jimenez from Manchester, United Kingdom

objects.title: Caricature
objects.extract: A caricature is a rendered image showing the features of its subject in a simplified or exaggerated way through sketching, pencil strokes, or other artistic drawings (compare to: cartoon). Caricatures can be either insulting or complimentary, and can serve a political purpose, be drawn solely for entertainment, or for a combination of both.

objects.title: John Deere
objects.description: American agricultural and industrial auto manufacturing corporation
objects.extract: Deere & Company (doing business as John Deere) (), is an American corporation that manufactures agricultural machinery, heavy equipment, forestry machinery, diesel engines, drivetrains (axles, transmissions, gearboxes) used in heavy equipment and lawn care equipment. It also provides financial services and other related activities.

objects.title: Monopoly (game)
objects.extract: Monopoly is a multiplayer economics-themed board game. In the game, players roll two standard dice (or one extra special red die depending on the game) to move their token clockwise around the game board, buying and trading properties and railroads and developing them with houses and hotels.

objects.title: Fire hydrant
objects.description: Connection point by which firefighters can tap into a water supply
objects.extract: A fire hydrant, fireplug, firecock (archaic), hydrant riser or Johnny Pump is a connection point by which firefighters can tap into a water supply. It is a component of active fire protection.

objects.title: Pectenoniscus montalvaniensis
objects.artist: Cardoso, Giovanna Monticelli et al. New cave species of Pectenoniscus Andersson,

objects.title: Phonograph record
objects.extract: A phonograph record (also known as a gramophone record, especially in British English) or a vinyl record (for later varieties only) is a disc-shaped analog sound storage medium with an inscribed, modulated spiral groove. The groove usually starts near the outside edge and ends near the center of the disc.

objects.title: Oyster pail
objects.extract: An oyster pail (also known as a paper pail,  Chinese food box or Chinese takeout container) is a folded, waxed or plastic coated, paperboard container originally designed to hold oysters. It commonly comes with a handle made of solid wire.

objects.title: Shield
objects.description: Item of armour carried to intercept attacks or projectiles
objects.extract: A shield is a piece of personal armour held in the hand, which may or may not be strapped to the wrist or forearm. Shields are used to intercept specific attacks, whether from close-ranged weaponry like spears or long ranged projectiles such as arrows.

objects.title: HP 95LX
objects.description: 1991 personal digital assistant from Hewlett Packard
objects.extract: The HP 95LX Palmtop PC (F1000A, F1010A), also known as project Jaguar, is Hewlett Packard's first DOS-based pocket computer, or personal digital assistant, introduced in April 1991 in collaboration with Lotus Development Corporation. The abbreviation "LX" stood for "Lotus Expandable".

objects.title: Jewellery
objects.extract: Jewellery (or jewelry in American English) consists of decorative items worn for personal adornment such as brooches, rings, necklaces, earrings, pendants, bracelets, and cufflinks. Jewellery may be attached to the body or the clothes.

objects.title: FOS.280
objects.description: Dorsetichthys bechei fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Hockey pants
objects.extract:
Hockey pants are knee-to-waist protective gear for ice hockey or roller hockey players. The pants carry a variety of padding depending on whether they are worn by goaltenders or skaters (forwards and
defenders), and also on the manufacturer.

objects.title: Caterpillar D10
objects.extract: The Caterpillar D10 is a bulldozer originally manufactured by Caterpillar between 1977 and 1987, when it was superseded by the D11; the successor of the smaller D9L was then rebranded as the D10N, which series of models continues to be produced (under the subsequent D10R, D10T, and—once again, as of 2023—D10 designations). It was the first modern tractor to

objects.title: Spharophon
objects.extract: The Sphärophon or a Spherophone is an electrical musical instrument that was first made as the "Electrophon" around 1921 by Jörg Mager, later modified, renamed and exhibited in 1926.

objects.title: Makuṭa
objects.description: Headdress used as crown in Southeast Asia
objects.extract: The makuṭa (Sanskrit: मुकुट), variously known in several languages as makuta, mahkota, magaik, mokot, mongkut or chada (see § Etymology and origins below), is a type of headdress used as crowns in the Southeast Asian monarchies of today's Cambodia and Thailand, and historically in Indonesia (Java, Sumatra, and Bali), Malaysia, Sri Lanka, Laos and Myanmar. Th

objects.title: Tubular bells
objects.extract: Tubular bells (also known as chimes) are a percussion instrument consisting of pitched metal tubes. These tubes are generally made of brass and may be chrome-plated.

objects.title: Relógio da Glória
objects.description: monument in the city of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

objects.title: Dessert
objects.extract: A dessert is a dish that consists of sweet foods, such as cake, biscuit, ice cream, and possibly a beverage, such as dessert wine or liqueur. Some cultures sweeten foods that are more commonly savory to create desserts.

objects.title: Kurī
objects.extract: The Kurī is an extinct breed of Polynesian Dog kept by Māori people. It was introduced to New Zealand by the Polynesian ancestors of the Māori during their migration from East Polynesia in the 13th century AD. According to Māori tradition, the demigod Māui transformed his brother-in-law Irawaru into the first dog.

objects.title: Hand fan
objects.extract: A handheld fan, or simply hand fan, is a broad, flat surface that is waved back and forth to create an airflow. Generally, purpose-made handheld fans are folding fans, which are shaped like a sector of a circle and made of a thin material (such as paper or feathers) mounted on slats which revolve around a pivot so that it can be closed when not in use.

objects.title: Footwear
objects.extract:
Footwear or footgear refers to garments worn on the feet, which typically serve the purpose of protection against adversities of the environment such as wear from rough ground; stability on slippery ground; and temperature.

Shoes and similar garments ease locomotion and prevent injuries.

objects.title: Dionysus
objects.description: Ancient Greek god of winemaking and wine
objects.extract: In ancient Greek religion and myth, Dionysus ( ; Ancient Greek: Διόνυσος Diónysos) is the god of wine-making, orchards, fruit, vegetation, fertility, festivity, insanity, ritual madness, religious ecstasy, and theatre. He was also known as Bacchus ( or ; Ancient Greek: Βάκχος Bacchos) by the Greeks (a name later adopted by the Romans) for a frenzy he is said

objects.title: United Airlines
objects.extract: United Airlines, Inc. is a major airline in the United States headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.

objects.title: Ushanka
objects.description: Russian fur cap with ear flaps
objects.extract:
An ushanka (Russian: ушанка, IPA: [ʊˈʂankə], from уши, ushi, 'ears'), also called an ushanka-hat (Russian: шапка-ушанка, romanized: shapka-ushanka, [ˈʂapkə ʊˈʂankə]), is a Russian fur hat with ear-covering flaps that can be tied up on the crown of the cap, or fastened at the chin to protect the ears, jaw, and lower chin from the cold.
An alternative way to w

objects.title: Bún bò Huế
objects.extract: Bún bò Huế (pronounced [ɓun˧˥ ɓɔ˧˩ hwe˧˥]) or bún bò (English: ) is a Vietnamese rice noodle (bún) dish with sliced beef (bò), chả lụa, and sometimes pork knuckles. The dish originates from Huế, a city in central Vietnam associated with the cooking style of the former royal court.

objects.title: Orientalism
objects.description: Imitation or depiction of Eastern cultures
objects.extract: In art history, literature, and cultural studies, Orientalism is the imitation or depiction of aspects of the Eastern world (or "Orient") by writers, designers, and artists from the Western world. Orientalist painting, particularly of West Asia and North Africa, was one of the many specialties of 19th-century academic art, and Western literature was influenc

objects.title: Amphibious vehicle
objects.description: Vehicle that works on land and on/under water
objects.extract: An amphibious vehicle (or simply amphibian) is a vehicle that works both on land and on or under water. Amphibious vehicles include amphibious bicycles, ATVs, cars, buses, trucks, railway vehicles, combat vehicles, and hovercraft.

objects.title: List of butter dishes
objects.extract: This is a list of notable butter dishes and foods in which butter is used as a primary ingredient or as a significant component of a dish or a food. Butter is a dairy product that consists of butterfat, milk proteins, and water.

objects.title: Harvesting knife (hu 笏) with mask and felines, fragment
objects.description: ceremonial object in the Freer Gallery of Art collection

objects.title: Newspaper
objects.description: Scheduled publication of information about current events
objects.extract: A newspaper is a periodical publication containing written information about current events and is often typed in black ink with a white or gray background. Newspapers can cover a wide variety of fields such as politics, business, sports, art, science, and religions.

objects.title: Dyseriocrania auricyanea
objects.extract: Dyseriocrania auricyanea is a moth of the  family Eriocraniidae. It was first described by Baron Walsingham in 1882 and is found in California.

objects.title: Pith helmet
objects.extract: The pith helmet, also known as the safari helmet, salacot, sola topee, sun helmet, topee, and topi, is a lightweight cloth-covered helmet made of sholapith. The pith helmet originates from the Spanish military adaptation of the native salakot headgear of the Philippines.

objects.title: Rhithrogena germanica
objects.extract: Rhithrogena germanica is a European species of mayfly, and is "probably the most famous of all British mayflies", because of its use in fly fishing. It is known in the British Isles as the March brown mayfly, a name which is used in the United States for a different species, Rhithrogena morrisoni.
objects.artist: Richard Bartz, Munich aka Makro Freak

objects.title: Fourth Crusade
objects.extract: The Fourth Crusade (1202–1204) was a Latin Christian armed expedition called by Pope Innocent III. The stated intent of the expedition was to recapture the Muslim-controlled city of Jerusalem, by first defeating the powerful Egyptian Ayyubid Sultanate. However, a sequence of economic and political events culminated in the Crusader army's 1202 siege of Zara a

objects.title: Swimfin
objects.description: Footwear used for personal propulsion through water
objects.extract: Swimfins, swim fins, diving fins, or flippers are finlike accessories worn on the feet, legs or hands  and made from rubber, plastic, carbon fiber or combinations of these materials, to aid movement through the water in water sports activities such as swimming, bodyboarding, bodysurfing, float-tube fishing, kneeboarding, riverboarding, scuba diving, snorkeli

objects.title: Concrete
objects.extract: Concrete is a composite material composed of aggregate bound together with a fluid cement that cures to a solid. It is the second-most-used substance (after water), the most widely used building material, and the most-manufactured material in the world.

objects.title: Chitty Bang Bang
objects.description: informal name of a number of celebrated English racing cars

objects.title: Dye laser
objects.description: Equipment using an organic dye to emit coherent light
objects.extract: A dye laser is a laser that uses an organic dye as the lasing medium, usually as a liquid solution. Compared to gases and most solid state lasing media, a dye can usually be used for a much wider range of wavelengths, often spanning 50 to 100 nanometers or more.

objects.title: Ravanne
objects.extract: The ravanne is a large tambourine-like instrument used in sega music of Mauritius. It is made out of goat skin called “lapo cabri” (in Mauritian Creole) which usually needs to be heated up before playing.

objects.title: Heidelberg Tun
objects.extract: The Heidelberg Tun (German: Großes Fass), or Great Heidelberg Tun, is an extremely large wine vat contained within the cellars of Heidelberg Castle. There have been four such barrels in the history of Heidelberg.

objects.title: Microchera albocoronata
objects.extract: The snowcap (Microchera albocoronata) is a species of hummingbird in the "emeralds", tribe Trochilini of subfamily Trochilinae. It is found in Costa Rica, Honduras, Nicaragua, and Panama.
objects.artist: Michael Woodruff from Spokane, Washington, USA

objects.title: NRP Zaire
objects.description: 1971 small patrol boat of the Portuguese Navy

objects.title: Camellia
objects.description: Genus of flowering plants in the tea family
objects.extract: Camellia (pronounced  or ) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Theaceae. They are found in tropical and subtropical areas in eastern and southern Asia, from the Himalayas east to Japan and Indonesia.

objects.title: Parking meter
objects.description: Device for collecting money to use a given parking space
objects.extract: A parking meter is a device used to collect money in exchange for the right to park a vehicle in a particular place for a limited amount of time. Parking meters can be used by municipalities as a tool for enforcing their integrated on-street parking policy, usually related to their traffic and mobility management policies, but are also used for revenue.

objects.title: Cheetah
objects.description: Large feline of the genus Acinonyx
objects.extract: The cheetah (Acinonyx jubatus) is a species of large feline and the only living member of the genus Acinonyx. The cheetah is a pursuit predator and the fastest land animal, capable of running at 93–104 km/h (58–65 mph); it has evolved for speed, having powerful hindlimb muscles, long limbs and a flexible spine.
objects.artist: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

objects.title: Roman Empire
objects.extract: The Roman Empire was a state that dominated the Mediterranean and much of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa during the classical period. The Roman Republic had previously conquered most of these territories, which later came under permanent single-person rule following Octavian's rise to power and the establishment of the Augustan Principate in 27 BC. B

objects.title: Musical ensemble
objects.extract: A musical ensemble, also known as a music group, musical group, or band, is a group of people who perform instrumental and/or vocal music, with the ensemble typically known by a distinct name. Some music ensembles consist solely of instrumentalists, such as the jazz quartet or the orchestra.

objects.title: Juggling
objects.extract: Juggling is a physical skill, performed by a juggler, involving the manipulation of objects for recreation, entertainment, art or sport. The most recognizable form of juggling is toss juggling.

objects.title: Dyseriocrania griseocapitella
objects.extract: Dyseriocrania griseocapitella (Chinquapin leaf-miner) is a moth of the  family Eriocraniidae. It is found from Nova Scotia to Florida, west to Illinois and Mississippi.

objects.title: Chinese furniture
