objects.extract: An inkwell is a small jar or container, often made of glass, porcelain, silver, brass, or pewter, used for holding ink in a place convenient for the person who is writing. The artist or writer dips the brush, quill, or dip pen into the inkwell as needed or uses the inkwell as the source for filling the reservoir of a fountain pen.
objects.artist: Tim Evanson from Cleveland Heights, Ohio, USA

objects.title: Icon
objects.description: Religious work of art in Christianity
objects.extract: An icon (from Ancient Greek  εἰκών (eikṓn) 'image, resemblance') is a religious work of art, most commonly a painting, in the cultures of the Eastern Orthodox, Oriental Orthodox, Catholic, and Lutheran churches. The most common subjects include Jesus, Mary, saints, and angels.

objects.title: elechek
objects.description: Kyrgyz traditional headwear with cap and turban-like white fabric adorned with embroidery, ribbons, and jewelry, integral to marriage ceremonies

objects.title: Human-powered transport
objects.description: Transport of goods and/or people only using human muscles
objects.extract: Human-powered transport is the transport of person(s) and/or goods (freight) using human muscle power. Unlike animal-powered transport, human-powered transport has existed since time immemorial in the form of walking, running and swimming, as well as small vehicles such as litters, rickshaws, wheelchairs and wheelbarrows.

objects.title: Javanese people
objects.extract: The Javanese (Javanese: ꦮꦺꦴꦁꦗꦮ, romanized: Wong Jåwå (in the ngoko register), ꦠꦶꦪꦁꦗꦮꦶ, Tiyang Jawi (in the krama register); Indonesian: Orang Jawa) are an Austronesian ethnic group native to the central and eastern part of Java island, which covers most of the administrative areas of the provinces of Central Java, East Java, and Special Region of Yogyakarta.

objects.title: Meat grinder
objects.extract: A  meat grinder (also called a "meat mincer" in the UK) is a kitchen appliance for mincing (UK) or grinding (US) meat, fish, vegetables and other foods, raw or cooked. It replaces tools like the mincing knife.

objects.title: Carousel
objects.extract: A carousel or carrousel (mainly North American English), merry-go-round (International English), or galloper (British English) is a type of amusement ride consisting of a rotating circular platform with seats for riders. The seats are traditionally in the form of rows of animal figures (usually horses) mounted on posts, many of which move up and down to simu

objects.title: Fourth Crusade
objects.extract: The Fourth Crusade (1202–1204) was a Latin Christian armed expedition called by Pope Innocent III. The stated intent of the expedition was to recapture the Muslim-controlled city of Jerusalem, by first defeating the powerful Egyptian Ayyubid Sultanate. However, a sequence of economic and political events culminated in the Crusader army's 1202 siege of Zara a

objects.title: Table tennis
objects.extract: Table tennis (also known as ping-pong) is a racket sport derived from tennis but distinguished by its playing surface being atop a stationary table, rather than the court on which players stand. Either individually or in teams of two, players take alternating turns returning a light, hollow ball over the table's net onto the opposing half of the court using

objects.title: Esparto
objects.extract: Esparto, halfah grass, or esparto grass is a fiber produced from two species of perennial grasses of north Africa,  Spain and Portugal. It is used for crafts, such as basketry, espadrilles and cords.

objects.title: Moses
objects.extract: In Abrahamic religions, Moses was the Hebrew prophet who led the Israelites out of slavery in the Exodus from Egypt. He is considered the most important prophet in Judaism and Samaritanism, and one of the most important prophets in Christianity, Islam, the Baháʼí Faith, and other Abrahamic religions.

objects.title: "BeeZy" BG-1000
objects.description: "BeeZy" (BG-1000), a hand-assembled Z80-based computer by Daniel Borel and Pierluigi Zappacosta, financed by Bobst Graphic from 1978 to March 1981

objects.title: Sombrero
objects.extract: The sombrero jarano, also known as sombrero de charro ('charro hat', referring to the traditional Mexican horsemen) and simply as sombrero in English, is a type of hat worn in Mexico. It features an extra-wide brim used to shield the face and eyes from the sun, that is slightly upturned at the edge; a usually high, conical, pointed crown; and a chin strap to

objects.title: Cake
objects.extract: Cake is a baker's confectionery made by combining flour with a liquid, a fat or both, and usually with a leavening agent. Cakes are usually baked, but can be griddled.

objects.title: List of doughnut varieties
objects.extract: Doughnuts are a type of fried dough food. The following is a list of doughnut varieties.

objects.title: Tape recorder
objects.description: Machine for recording sound onto magnetic tape
objects.extract: An audio tape recorder, also known as a tape deck, tape player, tape machine, or simply a tape recorder, is a sound recording and reproduction device that records and plays back sounds usually using magnetic tape for storage.  In its present-day form, it records a fluctuating signal by moving the tape across a tape head that polarizes the magnetic domains in
objects.artist: Franklin C. Goodale, of Tacoma, Washington 1908

objects.title: Campervan
objects.extract: A campervan, also referred to as a camper, caravanette, motorhome or RV (recreational vehicle) in North America, is a self-propelled vehicle that provides transport and sleeping accommodation, and usually cooking facilities. The term describes a van that has been fitted out, whereas a motorhome is one with a coachbuilt body.

objects.title: John Deere
objects.description: American agricultural and industrial auto manufacturing corporation
objects.extract: Deere & Company (doing business as John Deere) (), is an American corporation that manufactures agricultural machinery, heavy equipment, forestry machinery, diesel engines, drivetrains (axles, transmissions, gearboxes) used in heavy equipment and lawn care equipment. It also provides financial services and other related activities.

objects.title: Lynn, Massachusetts
objects.description: City in Essex County, Massachusetts, US
objects.extract: Lynn is the eighth-largest municipality in Massachusetts, United States, and the largest city in Essex County. Situated on the Atlantic Ocean, 3.7 miles (6.0 km) north of the Boston city line at Suffolk Downs, Lynn is part of Greater Boston's urban inner core and is a major economic and cultural center of the North Shore.

objects.title: Straight razor
objects.description: Knife used to remove body hair
objects.extract: A straight razor is a razor with a blade that can fold into its handle. They are also called open razors and cut-throat razors.

objects.title: Dessert
objects.extract: A dessert is a dish that consists of sweet foods, such as cake, biscuit, ice cream, and possibly a beverage, such as dessert wine or liqueur. Some cultures sweeten foods that are more commonly savory to create desserts.

objects.title: Acer campestre ʽCarnival’
objects.extract: The Field Maple Acer campestre cultivar 'Carnival' arose from a chance seedling discovered in the Netherlands in 1989.
objects.artist: Megan Hansen from Portland, OR, US

objects.title: PX1
objects.description: handheld backscatter X-ray scanner or imager 140keV

objects.title: Fast fashion
objects.description: Making fashion trends quickly and cheaply available to consumers
objects.extract: Fast fashion is a business model in textile manufacturing where companies quickly create and sell clothing and footwear at affordable prices by replicating the latest fashion trends and designs using cheap and fast mass production techniques. Fast fashion is defined by Merriam-Webster as "an approach to the design, creation, and marketing of clothing fashion

objects.title: Electric car
objects.description: Car propelled by an electric motor using energy stored in batteries
objects.extract: An electric car or electric vehicle (EV) is an automobile that uses electrical energy as the primary source of propulsion. The term normally refers to a plug-in electric vehicle, typically a battery electric vehicle (BEV), which only uses energy stored in on-board battery packs, but broadly may also include plug-in hybrid electric vehicle (PHEV), range-exten

objects.title: Goods
objects.description: Things or services that satisfy human wants
objects.extract: In economics, goods are anything that is good, usually in the sense that it provides welfare or utility to someone. Goods can be contrasted with bads, i.e.

objects.title: Kiwifruit
objects.description: Edible berries native to Northeast Asia
objects.extract: Kiwifruit (often shortened to kiwi), or Chinese gooseberry (traditional Chinese: 獼猴桃; simplified Chinese: 猕猴桃; pinyin: míhóutáo), is the edible berry of several species of woody vines in the genus Actinidia. The most common cultivar group of kiwifruit (Actinidia chinensis var.

objects.title: Camera phone
objects.extract: A camera phone is a mobile phone that is able to capture photographs and often record video using one or more built-in digital cameras. It can also send the resulting image wirelessly and conveniently.

objects.title: Twelve Olympians
objects.description: Major deities of the Greek pantheon
objects.extract: In ancient Greek religion and mythology, the twelve Olympians are the major deities of the Greek pantheon, commonly considered to be Zeus, Poseidon, Hera, Demeter, Aphrodite, Athena, Artemis, Apollo, Ares, Hephaestus, Hermes, and either Hestia or Dionysus. They were called Olympians because, according to tradition, they resided on Mount Olympus.

objects.title: Drum kit
objects.description: Musical instrument consisting of a collection of percussion instruments
objects.extract: A drum kit, acoustic drum kit or drum set (also known as a trap set or simply drums in popular music and jazz contexts) is a musical instrument consisting of a collection of percussion instruments arranged to be played by a single musician. It typically includes drums, cymbals, and sometimes other auxiliary percussion instruments, such as a tambourine or a c
objects.artist: Kamal Aboul-Hosn from New York, United States

objects.title: Lizard
objects.extract: Lizard is the common name used for all squamate reptiles other than snakes (and to a lesser extent amphisbaenians or "worm lizards"), encompassing over 7,000 species, ranging across all continents except Antarctica, as well as most oceanic island chains. The grouping is paraphyletic as some lizards are more closely related to snakes than they are to other li

objects.title: Flexible tank
objects.extract: Flexible tanks (also flexi-bags, flexibags and  flexi-tanks) are a kind of storage equipment for liquids such as water or oil. Compared to steel tanks, flexible tanks have many advantages, including lighter weight and being rustproof, foldable, and quicker and easier to set up.

objects.title: Pinaka
objects.extract: The Pinaka (Sanskrit: पिनाक, pināka) is the celestial bow of the Hindu deity, Shiva, which was crafted by Vishwakarma. In popular legend, he is believed to have employed this bow in his avatar as Tripurantaka to annihilate the three cities of Mayasura, known as Tripura.

objects.title: FOS.290
objects.description: Dapedium ovalis fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Safe
objects.description: Secure lockable box used for securing valuable objects
objects.extract: A safe (also called a strongbox or coffer) is a secure lockable enclosure used for securing valuable objects against theft or fire. A safe is usually a hollow cuboid or cylinder, with one face being removable or hinged to form a door.

objects.title: Schoemaker atlas
objects.artist: Andries Schoemaker / Gerrit Schoemaker / Cornelis Pronk / Abraham de Haen

objects.title: Air brake (road vehicle)
objects.description: Type of friction brake for vehicles
objects.extract: An air brake or, more formally, a compressed-air-brake system, is a type of friction brake for vehicles in which compressed air pressing on a piston is used to both release the parking/emergency brakes in order to move the vehicle, and also to apply pressure to the brake pads or brake shoes to slow and stop the vehicle.  Air brakes are used in large heavy ve

objects.title: Door and Frame
objects.description: furniture and woodwork by unknown artist (1916.2051)

objects.title: Sundial
objects.extract: A sundial is a horological device that tells the time of day (referred to as civil time in modern usage) when direct sunlight shines by the apparent position of the Sun in the sky. In the narrowest sense of the word, it consists of a flat plate (the dial) and a gnomon, which casts a shadow onto the dial.

objects.title: victory flower
objects.extract: Victoria cruziana (Santa Cruz water lily, water platter, yrupe, synonym Victoria argentina Burmeist.) is a tropical species of flowering plant, of the Nymphaeaceae family of water lilies native to South America, primarily Brazil, Bolivia, Argentina and Paraguay.

objects.title: Baboon
objects.extract: Baboons are primates comprising the genus Papio, one of the 23 genera of Old World monkeys, in the family Cercopithecidae. There are six species of baboon: the hamadryas baboon, the Guinea baboon, the olive baboon, the yellow baboon, the Kinda baboon and the chacma baboon.

objects.title: Quartz-MGL 90114
objects.description: sample of quartz crystals in hedgehog shape typical of Val Bedretto, found at Val Bedretto, Tessin, Switzerland

objects.title: Paper
objects.extract: Paper is a thin sheet of matted cellulose fibers. Largely derived from lignocellulose, paper is created from a pulp dissolved into a slurry that is drained and dried into sheets.

objects.title: Shellac
objects.description: Resin secreted by the female lac bug
objects.extract: Shellac () is a resin secreted by the female lac bug on trees in the forests of India and Thailand. Chemically, it is mainly composed of aleuritic acid, jalaric acid, shellolic acid, and other natural waxes.

objects.title: Coldstream Guards
objects.description: Infantry regiment of the British Army
objects.extract: The Coldstream Guards is the oldest continuously serving regular regiment in the British Army. As part of the Household Division, one of its principal roles is the protection of the monarchy; due to this, it often participates in state ceremonial occasions.

objects.title: Airedale Terrier
objects.extract: The Airedale Terrier (often shortened to "Airedale"), also called Bingley Terrier and Waterside Terrier, is a dog breed of the terrier type that originated in the valley (dale) of the River Aire, in the West Riding of Yorkshire, England. It is traditionally called the "King of Terriers" because it is the largest of the terrier breeds.

objects.title: Brittany
objects.extract: Brittany ( BRIT-ən-ee) is a peninsula, historical region and cultural area in the north-west of modern France, covering the western part of what was known as Armorica in Roman Gaul. It became an independent kingdom and then a duchy before being united with the Kingdom of France in 1532 as a province governed as a separate nation under the crown.

objects.title: Blender
objects.description: Kitchen appliance used to crush food or make liquid
objects.extract: A blender (sometimes called a mixer or liquidiser in British English) is a kitchen and laboratory appliance used to mix, crush, purée or emulsify food and other substances. A stationary blender consists of a blender container with a rotating metal or plastic  blade at the bottom, powered by an electric motor that is in the base.

objects.title: Hong Kong
objects.extract: Hong Kong is a special administrative region of China. Situated on China's southern coast just south of Shenzhen, it consists of Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, and the New Territories.

objects.title: Gas cylinder
objects.description: Cylindrical container for storing pressurised gas
objects.extract: A gas cylinder is a pressure vessel for storage and containment of gases at above atmospheric pressure. Gas storage cylinders may also be called bottles.

objects.title: Parking meter
objects.description: Device for collecting money to use a given parking space
objects.extract: A parking meter is a device used to collect money in exchange for the right to park a vehicle in a particular place for a limited amount of time. Parking meters can be used by municipalities as a tool for enforcing their integrated on-street parking policy, usually related to their traffic and mobility management policies, but are also used for revenue.

objects.title: Christmas lights
objects.extract: Christmas lights (also known as fairy lights, festive lights or string lights) are lights often used for decoration in celebration of Christmas, often on display throughout the Christmas season including Advent and Christmastide.  The custom goes back to when Christmas trees were decorated with candles, which symbolized Christ being the light of the world.

objects.title: Ice skate
objects.description: Boots with blades attached to the bottom for propelling the bearer across a sheet of ice
objects.extract:
Ice skates are metal blades attached underfoot and used to propel the bearer across a sheet of ice while ice skating.
The first ice skates were made from leg bones of horse, ox or deer, and were attached to feet with leather straps.

objects.title: Australian Defence Force
objects.extract: The Australian Defence Force (ADF) is the military organisation responsible for the defence of Australia and its national interests. It consists of three services: the Royal Australian Navy (RAN), the Australian Army and the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF).
objects.artist: U.S. Navy Photo By Mass Communication Specialist 2nd Class Jeff Troutman

objects.title: Pottery
objects.description: Craft of making objects from clay
objects.extract: Pottery is the process and the products of forming vessels and other objects with clay and other raw materials, which are fired at high temperatures to give them a hard and durable form. The place where a potter makes such wares is also called a pottery (plural potteries).

objects.title: Dump truck
objects.description: Truck which can tip its bed, dumping its contents
objects.extract: A dump truck, known also as a dumping truck, dump lorry or dumper lorry or a dumper for short, is used for transporting materials (such as dirt, gravel, or demolition waste) for construction as well as coal. A typical dump truck is equipped with an open-box bed, which is hinged at the rear and equipped with hydraulic rams to lift the front, allowing the mate

objects.title: Papal tiara
objects.description: Crown worn by popes of the Roman Catholic Church
objects.extract: The papal tiara is a crown that was worn by popes of the Catholic Church from as early as the 8th century to the mid–20th century. It was last used by Pope Paul VI in 1963, and only at the beginning of his reign.

objects.title: Wagon
objects.description: Four wheeled vehicle pulled by draft animals
objects.extract:
A wagon (or waggon) is a heavy four-wheeled vehicle pulled by draft animals or on occasion by humans, used for transporting goods, commodities, agricultural materials, supplies and sometimes people.
Wagons are immediately distinguished from carts (which have two wheels) and from lighter four-wheeled vehicles primarily for carrying people, such as carriages.

objects.title: Hose clamp
objects.description: Device used to attach and seal a hose onto a fitting
objects.extract: A hose clamp is a device used to attach and seal a hose onto a fitting such as a barb or nipple.

objects.title: Balloon (aeronautics)
objects.description: Type of aerostat that remains aloft due to its buoyancy
objects.extract: In aeronautics, a balloon is an unpowered aerostat, which remains aloft or floats due to its buoyancy. It may use hot air as a lifting gas, or it may use gas that is not air like hydrogen or helium.

objects.title: Room 312, display case 1
objects.description: Display 1 of room 312 in the Louvre: the Phenician kingdoms

objects.title: Cheddar cheese
objects.extract: Cheddar cheese (or simply cheddar) is a natural cheese that is relatively hard, off-white (or orange if colourings such as annatto are added), and sometimes sharp-tasting. It originates from the village of Cheddar in Somerset, South West England.

objects.title: Orchid
objects.description: Family of flowering plants in the order Asparagales
objects.extract: Orchids are plants that belong to the family Orchidaceae (), a diverse and widespread group of flowering plants with blooms that are often colourful and fragrant. Orchids are cosmopolitan plants, living in diverse habitats on every continent except Antarctica.

objects.title: Sedan (automobile)
objects.description: Passenger car in a three-box configuration
objects.extract: A sedan (American English), or saloon (British English), is a passenger car in a three-box configuration with separate compartments for an engine, passengers, and cargo. Variations of the sedan style include the close-coupled sedan, club sedan, convertible sedan, fastback sedan, hardtop sedan, notchback sedan, and sedanet.

objects.title: Tennis ball
objects.description: Ball used in the sport of tennis
objects.extract: A tennis ball is a small, hollow ball used in games of tennis and real tennis. Tennis balls are fluorescent yellow in professional competitions, but in recreational play other colors are also used.

objects.title: Coral reef
objects.description: Outcrop of rock in the sea formed by the growth and deposit of stony coral skeletons
objects.extract: A coral reef is an underwater ecosystem characterized by reef-building corals. Reefs are formed of colonies of coral polyps held together by calcium carbonate.

objects.title: Korean Air Lines Flight 007
objects.description: 1983 aircraft shotdown over the Sea of Japan
objects.extract: Korean Air Lines Flight 007 was a scheduled Korean Air Lines flight from New York City to Seoul via Anchorage. On September 1, 1983, the flight was shot down by a Soviet Sukhoi Su-15 interceptor aircraft.

objects.title: Moth
objects.description: Group of mostly-nocturnal insects in the order Lepidoptera
objects.extract: Moths are a group of winged insect of the order Lepidoptera, the other well-known members of which are butterflies, they are characterized by dark or dull patterned wings. They were previously classified as suborder Heterocera, but the group is paraphyletic with respect to butterflies (suborder Rhopalocera) and neither subordinate taxon is used in modern cla

objects.title: V-1 flying bomb
objects.description: German World War II cruise missile
objects.extract: The V-1 flying bomb (German: Vergeltungswaffe 1, lit. 'Vengeance Weapon 1') was an early cruise missile. Its official Reich Aviation Ministry (RLM) name was Fieseler Fi 103 and its suggestive name was Höllenhund (hellhound).

objects.title: Folk art
objects.description: Art produced from an indigenous culture or by peasants or other laboring tradespeople
objects.extract: Folk art  covers all forms of visual art made in the context of folk culture.  Definitions vary, but generally the objects have practical utility of some kind, rather than being exclusively decorative.

objects.title: List of Japanese desserts and sweets
objects.extract: The Japanese had been making desserts for centuries before sugar was widely available in Japan. Many desserts commonly available in Japan can be traced back hundreds of years.

objects.title: Cornet
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (F117)

objects.title: Ancient Semitic-speaking peoples
objects.description: Residents of the ancient Near East until the end of antiquity
objects.extract: Ancient Semitic-speaking peoples or Proto-Semitic people were speakers of Semitic languages who lived throughout the ancient Near East and later also  North Africa, including the Levant, Mesopotamia, Anatolia, the Arabian Peninsula and Carthage from the 3rd millennium BC until the end of antiquity, with some, such as Arabs, Arameans, Assyrians, Jews, Mandaea

objects.title: Typewriter
objects.extract: A typewriter is a mechanical or electromechanical machine for typing characters. Typically, a typewriter has an array of keys, and each one causes a different single character to be produced on paper by striking an inked ribbon selectively against the paper with a type element.
objects.artist: Illustrator: UnknownPatent assignees: Christopher L. Sholes, Carlos Glidden, and

objects.title: Saddle
objects.description: Supportive structure for a rider or load
objects.extract: A saddle is a supportive structure for a rider of an animal, fastened to an animal's back by a girth. The most common type is equestrian.

objects.title: Artificial leather
objects.extract: Artificial leather, also called synthetic leather, is a material intended to substitute for leather in upholstery, clothing, footwear, and other uses where a leather-like finish is desired but the actual material is cost-prohibitive or unsuitable due to practical or ethical concerns. Artificial leather is known by many names, including leatherette, imitation

objects.title: Jesus
objects.description: First-century Jewish preacher and religious leader
objects.extract: Jesus (c. 6 to 4 BC – AD 30 or 33), also referred to as Jesus Christ, Jesus of Nazareth, and by various other names and titles, was a 1st-century Jewish preacher and religious leader in the Roman province of Judaea. He is the central figure of Christianity, the world's largest religion.

objects.title: Americas
objects.description: Landmass comprising North and South America
objects.extract: The Americas, sometimes collectively called America, are a landmass comprising the totality of North America and South America. When viewed as a single continent, the Americas are the second largest continent by area after Asia and the third largest continent by population.

objects.title: Dessert
objects.extract: A dessert is a dish that consists of sweet foods, such as cake, biscuit, ice cream, and possibly a beverage, such as dessert wine or liqueur. Some cultures sweeten foods that are more commonly savory to create desserts.

objects.title: Tankard
objects.extract: A tankard is a form of drinkware consisting of a large, roughly cylindrical, drinking cup with a single handle. In recent centuries tankards were typically made of silver or pewter, but they can be made of other materials, for example glass, wood, pottery, or boiled leather.

objects.title: Cigarette pack
objects.extract: A pack or packet of cigarettes (also informally called fag packet in British slang; as in the idiom "back of a fag packet" or "fag-packet calculation") is a rectangular container, mostly of paperboard, which contains cigarettes. The pack is designed with a flavor-protective foil, paper or plastic, and sealed through a transparent airtight plastic film.

objects.title: African buffalo
objects.extract: The African buffalo (Syncerus caffer) is a large species of true buffalo native to Sub-Saharan Africa, where it is found in a number of disconnected ranges stretching from south-eastern Senegal through West and Central Africa to South Africa. The adult African buffalo's horns are its characteristic feature: they have fused bases that form a continuous bone s

objects.title: Funicular
objects.extract: A funicular ( few-NIK-yoo-lər, f(y)uu-, f(j)ə-), or funicular railway, is a type of cable railway system that connects points along a railway track laid on a steep slope. The system is characterized by two counterbalanced carriages (also called cars or trains) permanently attached to opposite ends of a haulage cable, which is looped over a pulley at the uppe

objects.title: IBM PC compatible
objects.description: Computers derived from the IBM PC
objects.extract: An IBM PC compatible is any personal computer that is hardware- and software-compatible with the IBM Personal Computer (IBM PC) and its successors. Like the original IBM PC, an IBM PC–compatible computer uses an x86-based central processing unit, sourced either from Intel or a second source like AMD, Cyrix or other vendors such as Texas Instruments, Fujitsu,

objects.title: Greater India
objects.description: Cultural sphere of India beyond the Indian subcontinent
objects.extract: Greater India, also known as the Indian cultural sphere, the Indic world, or the Hindu Cultural Sphere is an area composed of several countries and regions in South Asia, East Asia and Southeast Asia that were historically influenced by Indian culture, which itself formed from the various distinct indigenous cultures of the Indian subcontinent. It is an umbr

objects.title: East–West Schism
objects.description: Break of communion between the Western and Eastern churches
objects.extract: The East–West Schism, also known as the Great Schism or the Schism of 1054, is the break of communion between the Catholic Church and the Eastern Orthodox Church since 1054. A series of ecclesiastical differences, theological disputes and geopolitical tensions between the Greek East and Latin West preceded the formal split in 1054.

objects.title: Rope
objects.extract: A rope is a group of yarns, plies, fibres, or strands that are twisted or braided together into a larger and stronger form. Ropes have high tensile strength and can be used for dragging and lifting.

objects.title: Beech
objects.description: Genus of trees in the family Fagaceae
objects.extract: Beech (genus Fagus) is a genus of deciduous trees in the family Fagaceae, native to subtropical (accessory forest element) and temperate (as dominant element of mesophytic forests) Eurasia and North America. There are 14 accepted species in two distinct subgenera, Englerianae Denk & G.W.Grimm and Fagus.

objects.title: Tommaso da Modena
objects.extract: Tomaso Barisini, better known as Tommaso da Modena and sometimes called Tomaso Baffini (1326 – 1379) was an Italian painter of the mid-14th century.

objects.title: Gastropholis prasina
objects.extract: Gastropholis prasina, the green keel-bellied lizard, is a species of lizard belonging to the family Lacertidae.

objects.title: Array mbira
objects.extract: The Array mbira  is a handcrafted modern musical instrument with a unique harp- or bell-like sound. It is made in the United States by its inventor Bill Wesley and manufactured by Wesley with Patrick Hadley in San Diego, California, United States.

objects.title: Belt (clothing)
objects.description: Worn band or braid, usually around the waist or hips
objects.extract: A belt is a flexible band or strap, typically made of leather, plastic, or heavy cloth, worn around the natural waist or near it (as far down as the hips). The ends of a belt are free, and a buckle is often used to form the belt into a loop by securing one end to another part of the belt, at or near the other end, though they may also be without a buckle and

objects.title: Pancake
objects.description: Thin round cake made of eggs, milk, and flour
objects.extract: A pancake, also known as a hotcake, griddlecake, or flapjack, is a flat type of batter bread like cake, often thin and round, prepared from a starch-based batter that may contain eggs, milk, and butter, and then cooked on a hot surface such as a griddle or frying pan. Archaeological evidence suggests that pancakes were probably eaten in prehistoric societies

objects.title: Lisle Corporation
objects.extract: Lisle Corporation is an American manufacturer of auto mechanic's specialty tools. It is an independent, private corporation that has been operated in Clarinda, Iowa by members of the Lisle family since its founding in 1903.

objects.title: Movie camera
objects.description: Special type of camera used to shoot movies
objects.extract: A movie camera (also known as a motion picture camera, or cine-camera) is a type of photographic camera that rapidly takes a sequence of photographs, either onto film stock or an image sensor, in order to produce a moving image to display on a screen. In contrast to the still camera, which captures a single image at a time, the movie camera takes a series of

objects.title: Woodworking
objects.description: Process of making objects from wood
objects.extract: Woodworking is the skill of making items from wood, and includes cabinetry, furniture making, wood carving, joinery, carpentry, and woodturning.
objects.artist: Jeff Easter from San Francisco, America

objects.title: Sapele
objects.description: 250-seater passenger boat which is used for Circular Trips in London from Westminster, Embankment and Festival Pier

objects.title: Sugar candy
objects.extract: Sugar candy (American English) or sweets (British English) is any candy whose primary ingredient is sugar. The main types of sugar candies are hard candies, fondants, caramels, jellies, and nougats.

objects.title: 1970s in fashion
objects.extract: Fashion in the 1970s was about individuality. In the early 1970s, Vogue proclaimed "There are no rules in the fashion game now" due to overproduction flooding the market with affordable synthetic clothing.

objects.title: Louis XV style
objects.extract: The Louis XV style or Louis Quinze (, French: [lwi kɛ̃z]) is a style of architecture and decorative arts which appeared during the reign of Louis XV. From 1710 until about 1730, a period known as the Régence, it was largely an extension of the Louis XIV style of his great-grandfather and predecessor, Louis XIV. From about 1730 until about 1750, it became mor

objects.title: Walters Art Museum
objects.description: Art museum in Baltimore, Maryland, US
objects.extract: The Walters Art Museum is a public art museum located in the Mount Vernon neighborhood of Baltimore, Maryland. Founded and opened in 1934, it holds collections from the mid-19th century that were  amassed substantially by major American art and sculpture collectors, including William Thompson Walters and his son Henry Walters.

objects.title: Flying disc freestyle
objects.extract: Flying disc freestyle, also known as freestyle Frisbee in reference to the trademarked brand name, is a sport and performing art characterized by creative, acrobatic, and athletic maneuvers with a flying disc. Freestyle is performed individually or more commonly in groups, both competitively and recreationally.

objects.title: Vessel rattle
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (F645)

objects.title: Snowcat
objects.description: Enclosed-cab, truck-sized, fully tracked vehicle designed for over-snow travel
objects.extract: A snowcat (a portmanteau of snow and caterpillar) is an enclosed-cab, truck-sized, fully tracked vehicle designed to travel over snow. Major manufacturers are PistenBully (Germany), Prinoth (Italy), and Tucker (United States).

objects.title: Lady Gaga
objects.extract: Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta (born March 28, 1986), known professionally as Lady Gaga, is an American singer, songwriter, and actress. Known for her image reinventions and versatility across the entertainment industry, she is an influential figure in popular music.

objects.title: Fragment of a culverin
objects.description: part of the collection of Museum Halle Gate

objects.title: Cabinetry
objects.description: Box-shaped piece of furniture with doors
objects.extract: A cabinet is a case or cupboard with shelves or drawers for storing or displaying items. Some cabinets are stand alone while others are built in to a wall or are attached to it like a medicine cabinet.

objects.title: Oscillating multi-tool
objects.description: Power tool which works by oscillation
objects.extract: An oscillating multi-tool or oscillating saw is a multitool and power tool that oscillates (rather than rotating or reciprocating), powered by battery or mains. The name "multi-tool" is a reference to the many functions that this tool can perform with the range of attachments available.

objects.title: Salad
objects.description: Food mixture, served chilled or at room temperature
objects.extract: A salad is a dish consisting of mixed ingredients, frequently vegetables. They are typically served chilled or at room temperature, though some can be served warm.
objects.artist:
fir0002  flagstaffotos [at] gmail.com
		Canon 20D + Canon 17-40mm f/4 L

objects.title: Cross-stitch
objects.extract: Cross-stitch is a form of sewing and a popular form of counted-thread embroidery in which X-shaped stitches (called cross stitches) in a tiled, raster-like pattern are used to form a picture. The stitcher counts the threads on a piece of evenweave fabric (such as linen) in each direction so that the stitches are of uniform size and appearance.

objects.title: M1 Abrams
objects.extract: The M1 Abrams () is a third-generation American main battle tank designed by Chrysler Defense (now General Dynamics Land Systems) and named for General Creighton Abrams. Conceived for modern armored ground warfare, it is one of the heaviest tanks in service at nearly 73.6 short tons (66.8 metric tons).
objects.artist: U.S. Marine Corps photo by Sgt. Micha Pierce

objects.title: gözleme
objects.extract: Gözleme is a savory Turkish stuffed turnover. The dough is usually unleavened, and made only with flour, salt and water, but gözleme can be made from yeast dough as well.

objects.title: Wiseana cervinata
objects.extract: Wiseana cervinata, a porina moth, is a species of moth belonging to the family Hepialidae. It was described by Francis Walker in 1865 and is endemic to New Zealand.
objects.artist: Birgit E. Rhode, Landcare Research New Zealand Ltd.

objects.title: Pop pop boat
objects.description: Simple toy working on a water jet principle
objects.extract: A pop-pop boat (also known as a flash-steamer, hot-air-boat, or toc-toc after a German version from the 1920s) is a toy with a simple steam engine without moving parts, typically powered by a candle or vegetable oil burner. The name comes from the noise made by some versions of the boats.

objects.title: Amar'e Stoudemire
objects.extract: Amar'e Carsares Stoudemire (born November 16, 1982) is an American-Israeli professional basketball coach and former player who most recently served as a player development assistant for the Brooklyn Nets of the National Basketball Association (NBA). He was inducted into the International Jewish Sports Hall of Fame in 2025 and will be inducted into the Naismi

objects.title: Handbag
objects.description: Handled bag used to carry personal items
objects.extract: A handbag, commonly known as a purse in North American English, is a handled medium-to-large bag used to carry personal items.

objects.title: Laptop
objects.extract: A laptop is a portable personal computer (PC). Laptops typically have a clamshell form factor with a flat-panel screen on the inside of the upper lid and an alphanumeric keyboard and pointing device on the inside of the lower lid.
objects.artist: Tony Alter from Newport News, USA

objects.title: Clothes dryer
objects.description: Appliance used for drying wet clothes
objects.extract:
A clothes dryer (tumble dryer, drying machine, drying device, or simply dryer) is a powered household appliance that is used to remove moisture from a load of clothing, bedding and other textiles, usually after they are washed in the washing machine.
Many dryers consist of a rotating drum called a "tumbler" through which heated air is circulated to evaporate
objects.artist:
Original photo: Jerry on Flickr

objects.title: White wine
objects.extract: White wine is a wine that is fermented without undergoing the process of maceration, which involves prolonged contact between the juice and the grape skins, seeds, and pulp. The wine color can be straw-yellow, yellow-green, or yellow-gold.

objects.title: Straight razor
objects.description: Knife used to remove body hair
objects.extract: A straight razor is a razor with a blade that can fold into its handle. They are also called open razors and cut-throat razors.

objects.title: Scooter (motorcycle)
objects.extract:
A scooter or motor scooter, is a motorcycle with an underbone or step-through frame, a seat, and a platform for the rider's feet. 
Many scooters typically have a design that focuses on an easy method of operation and fuel economy.

objects.title: Bordeaux wine
objects.description: Wine produced in the Bordeaux region of France
objects.extract: Bordeaux wine (Occitan: vin de Bordèu; French: vin de Bordeaux) is produced in the Bordeaux region of southwest France, around the city of Bordeaux, on the Garonne River. To the north of the city, the Dordogne River joins the Garonne forming the broad estuary called the Gironde; the Gironde department, with a total vineyard area of 110,800 hectares, is the s

objects.title: Viola (plant)
objects.extract: Viola, commonly known as the violets, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Violaceae. It is the largest genus in the family, containing over 680 species.

objects.title: Vehicle
objects.description: Mobile equipment that transports people, animals or cargo
objects.extract: A vehicle (from Latin  vehiculum) is a machine designed for self-propulsion, usually to transport people, cargo, or both. The term "vehicle" typically refers to ground transport vehicles such as human-powered land vehicles (e.g.

objects.title: Orange wine
objects.description: Made of white wine grapes with the skins
objects.extract: Orange wine, also known as skin-contact white wine, skin-fermented white wine, or amber wine, is a type of wine made from white wine grapes where the grape skins are not removed but stay in contact with the juice for days or even months, as is more typical with red wines. This contrasts with conventional white wine production, which involves crushing the gra

objects.title: Species
objects.description: Basic unit of taxonomic classification, below genus
objects.extract: A species (pl. species) is the basic unit of classification and a taxonomic rank of an organism, as well as a unit of biodiversity. It can be defined as the largest group of organisms in which any two individuals of the appropriate sexes or mating types can produce fertile offspring, typically by sexual reproduction.

objects.title: Vehicle extrication
objects.description: Process of removing a person trapped in a vehicle
objects.extract: Vehicle extrication is the process of removing a patient from a vehicle which has been involved in a motor vehicle collision. Patients who have not already exited a crashed vehicle may be medically (cannot exit a vehicle due to their injuries) or physically trapped, or unable to exit the vehicle because a door will not open.

objects.title: Masquerade ball
objects.description: Event in which participants attend in costume wearing a mask
objects.extract: A masquerade ball (or bal masqué) is a special kind of formal ball which many participants attend in costume wearing masks. (Compare the word "masque"—a formal written and sung court pageant.) Less formal "costume parties" may be a descendant of this tradition.

objects.title: Wearable technology
objects.description: Clothing and accessories incorporating computer and advanced electronic technologies
objects.extract: Wearable technology is a category of small electronic and mobile devices with wireless communications capability designed to be worn on the human body and are incorporated into gadgets, accessories, or clothes. Common types of wearable technology include smartwatches, fitness trackers, and smartglasses.

objects.title: Royal Canadian Mounted Police
objects.extract: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP; French: Gendarmerie royale du Canada, GRC) is the national police service of Canada. The RCMP is an agency of the Government of Canada; it also provides police services under contract to 11 provinces and territories (all but Ontario and Quebec), over 150 municipalities, and 600 Indigenous communities.

objects.title: Computer
objects.extract: A computer is a machine that can be programmed to automatically carry out sequences of arithmetic or logical operations (computation). Modern digital electronic computers can perform generic sets of operations known as programs, which enable computers to perform a wide range of tasks.

objects.title: Girolamo dai Libri
objects.extract:
Girolamo dai Libri (1474/1475 – July 2, 1555) was an Italian illuminator of manuscripts and painter of altarpieces, working in an early Renaissance style. 
He learned the art from his father Francesco dai Libri, a skilled illuminator, but most likely also attended the workshop of Domenico Morone, where he formed a friendship with Morone's son Francesco, with

objects.title: Lady Gaga
objects.extract: Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta (born March 28, 1986), known professionally as Lady Gaga, is an American singer, songwriter, and actress. Known for her image reinventions and versatility across the entertainment industry, she is an influential figure in popular music.

objects.title: Candy making
objects.description: Preparation and cookery of candies and sugar confections
objects.extract: Candy making is the preparation and cookery of candies and sugar confections.  Candy making includes the preparation of many various candies, such as hard candies, jelly beans, gumdrops, taffy, liquorice, cotton candy, chocolates and chocolate truffles, dragées, fudge, caramel candy, and toffee.

objects.title: Termite
objects.extract: Termites are a group of eusocial Cockroaches which consume a variety of decaying plant material, generally in the form of wood, leaf litter, and soil humus. They are distinguished by their beaded (moniliform) antennae and the soft-bodied, unpigmented worker caste for which they have been commonly termed "white ants"; however, they are not ants but highly der

objects.title: Waterpot in form of a fruiting peach branch
Cabbage roll
objects.description: Dish of cabbage leaves with a filling
objects.extract: A cabbage roll is a dish consisting of cooked cabbage leaves wrapped around a variety of fillings. It is common to the cuisines of Central, Eastern and Southeastern Europe and much of Western Asia, Northern China, and parts of North Africa.

objects.title: Dance
objects.description: Art form consisting of body movement
objects.extract: Dance is an art form, consisting of sequences of body movements with aesthetic and often symbolic value, either improvised or purposefully selected. Dance can be categorized and described by its choreography, by its repertoire of movements or by its historical period or place of origin.

objects.title: Bull trout
objects.extract: The bull trout (Salvelinus confluentus) is a char of the family Salmonidae native to northwestern North America. Historically, S. confluentus has been known as the "Dolly Varden" (S. malma), but was reclassified as a separate species in 1980.

objects.title: Head shaving
objects.description: Shaving the hair from a person's head
objects.extract: Head shaving, or bald by choice, is the shaving of the hair from a person's head. People throughout history have shaved all or part of their heads for diverse reasons including aesthetics, convenience, culture, fashion, practicality, punishment, a rite of passage, religion, or style.

objects.title: Golf cart
objects.description: Small vehicle designed originally to carry golfers
objects.extract: A golf cart, golf buggy, or golf car, is a small motorized vehicle designed originally to carry two golfers and their golf clubs around a golf course with less effort than walking. Over time, variants were introduced that were capable of carrying more passengers, had additional utility features, or were certified as a street legal low-speed vehicle.

objects.title: Inline skates
objects.description: Boots with wheels arranged in a single line
objects.extract: Inline skates are boots with wheels arranged in a single line from front to back, allowing one to move in an ice skate-like fashion. Inline skates are a type of roller skate.

objects.title: Dango
objects.extract: Dango (団子) is a Japanese dumpling made with regular rice flour and glutinous rice flour. They are usually made in round shapes, and three to five pieces are served on a skewer, which is called kushi-dango (串団子).

objects.title: Nail gun
objects.extract: A nail gun, nailgun or nailer is a form of hammer used to drive nails into wood or other materials. It is usually driven by compressed air (pneumatic), electromagnetism, highly flammable gases such as butane or propane, or, for powder-actuated tools, a small explosive charge.

objects.title: Morris dance
objects.extract: Morris dancing is a form of English folk dance. It is based on rhythmic stepping and the execution of choreographed figures by a group of dancers in costume, usually wearing bell pads on their shins, their shoes or both.

objects.title: Machine gun
objects.extract: A machine gun (MG) is a fully automatic and rifled firearm designed for sustained direct fire. Automatic firearms of 20 mm (0.79 in) caliber or more are usually classified as autocannons rather than machine guns.

objects.title: Steam locomotive
objects.description: Railway locomotive that produces its pulling power through a steam engine
objects.extract: A steam locomotive is a type of locomotive that provides the force to move itself and other vehicles by means of the expansion of steam. It is fuelled by burning combustible material (usually coal, oil or, rarely, wood) to heat water in the locomotive's boiler to the point where it becomes gaseous and its volume increases 1,600 to 1,700 times.

objects.title: Frank Gehry
objects.extract: Frank Owen Gehry ( GAIR-ee; né Goldberg; February 28, 1929 – December 5, 2025) was a Canadian and American architect and designer known for his postmodern designs and use of unconventional forms and materials. A number of his buildings, including his private residence in Santa Monica, California, have become attractions.

objects.title: Dieter Rams
objects.extract: Dieter Rams (born 20 May 1932) is a German industrial designer who is most closely associated with the consumer products company Braun, the furniture company Vitsœ, and the functionalist school of industrial design. His unobtrusive approach and belief in "less, but better" (German: Weniger, aber besser) design has influenced the practice of design, as well a

objects.title: Salmon as food
objects.extract: Salmon is a common food fish classified as an oily fish with a rich content of protein and omega-3 fatty acids. Norway is a major producer of farmed and wild salmon, accounting for more than 50% of global salmon production.

objects.title: Wedding dress
objects.description: Dress worn by a bride during the wedding ceremony
objects.extract: A wedding dress or bridal gown is the dress worn by the bride during a wedding ceremony. The color, style and ceremonial importance of the gown can depend on the religion and culture of the wedding participants.

objects.title: Menat
objects.extract: In ancient Egyptian religion, a menat (Ancient Egyptian: mnj.t (𓏠𓈖𓇋𓏏𓋧), Arabic: منات) was a necklace closely associated with the goddess Hathor.

objects.title: Chewing gum
objects.description: Soft substance meant to be chewed without swallowing
objects.extract: Chewing gum is a soft, cohesive substance designed to be chewed without being swallowed. Modern chewing gum is composed of gum base, sweeteners, softeners/plasticizers, flavors, colors, and, typically, a hard or powdered polyol coating.

objects.title: wagasi
objects.extract: Wagasi is a type of West African cheese made from cow's milk. It is commonly made in northern Benin.

objects.title: Closed-loop box reuse
objects.extract: Closed-loop box reuse, is the process by which boxes or other containers are reused many times.  It is a form of reusable packaging.

objects.title: Bombus bohemicus
objects.extract: Bombus bohemicus, also known as the gypsy's cuckoo bumblebee, is a species of socially parasitic cuckoo bumblebee found in most of Europe with the exception of the southern Iberian Peninsula and Iceland. B. bohemicus practices inquilinism, or brood parasitism, of other bumblebee species.

objects.title: Truss
objects.description: Rigid structure that consists of two-force members only
objects.extract:
A truss is an assembly of members such as beams, connected by nodes, that creates a rigid structure.
In engineering, a truss is a structure that "consists of two-force members only, where the members are organized so that the assemblage as a whole behaves as a single object".

objects.title: Recumbent bicycle
objects.extract: A recumbent bicycle is a bicycle that places the rider in a laid-back reclining position, and often called a human-powered vehicle or HPV, especially if it has an aerodynamic fairing. Recumbents are available in a wide range of configurations, including: long to short wheelbase; large, small, or a mix of wheel sizes; overseat, underseat, or no-hands steering

objects.title: Pinball
objects.extract: Pinball games are a family of games in which a ball is propelled into a specially designed table where it bounces off various obstacles, scoring points either en-route or when it comes to rest. Historically the board was studded with nails called 'pins' and had hollows or pockets which scored points if the ball came to rest in them.

objects.title: High chair
objects.extract: A high chair is a piece of furniture used for feeding older babies and younger toddlers. The seat is raised a fair distance from the ground, so that a person of adult height may spoon-feed the child comfortably from a standing position (hence the name).

objects.title: Table saw
objects.extract: A table saw (also known as a sawbench or bench saw in England) is a woodworking tool, consisting of a circular saw blade, mounted on an arbor, that is driven by an electric motor (directly, by belt, by cable, or by gears). The drive mechanism is mounted below a table that provides support for the material, usually wood, being cut, with the blade protruding u

objects.title: Kerfuś
objects.description: Robot mascot of Carrefour in Poland
objects.extract: Kerfuś (Polish: [ˈkɛr.fuɕ]) is a mascot of the Carrefour store chain in Poland, in the form of an autonomous delivery robot distributing snacks, with the face of a cartoon cat. The mascot was introduced in stores in Warsaw, Poland, in 2022, as an advertisement campaign in the collaboration between Carrefour and PepsiCo.
